i was a bit scared purchasing this bc of the top they normally don’t fit my chest since i’m bigger (38G) but it’s very stretchy and at times i’d have some under boob but wasn’t a big deal and didn’t bother me. the bottoms rolled on the sides but that comes w being a little chubby. i like the bathing suit a lot overall.i did take the padding out of the top tho gave it a weird shape.

I got an XL. I'm 5'6", about 167lbs, dress size 8, measurements are 42" bust, 29.5" waist, 43" hips/butt, and a cup size 34H. Im DEFINITELY curvy and struggle immensely with bikinis because of my natural bust. I took a chance on this bikini and I was really impressed! The top was more than big enough to hold and cover my chest. Although, as predicted, strapless it struggled to support my 20lbs breasts. I could get away with it, even jump without them flying out. But it just wasnt the look I wanted. The straps were ok in their normal position, definitely held me better. I got creative and criss crossed the straps in the back. Which made it tighter, slightly more supportive, and added some cute flair. Also tried criss crossing over the front and that was also very cute but I wanted to avoid weird tan lines on my chest. The adjustability of this bikini is awesome! Of course after moving around my breasts would slide down and I'd have to adjust periodically but that's honestly just part of have big heavy boobs so I dont really fault the bikini. I DEFINITELY took out the padding because it looked bizarre on my body. But amazingly, my nipples didnt poke through all that much. The bottom fit like a dream, although it is tastefully cheeky. The waist was a smidge too big for me but frankly I'm used to that since I have such a huge difference between my waist and hips and some pudge. For the price it's awesome. Fabric is very comfy. The color is so gorgeous! Its vibrant and bright, exactly what I wanted. For an Amazon bikini it's very nice. Supposedly you cannot machine wash it? Which is a downer but eh there are worse things. Overall, if you're curvy, busty, big booty, have no fear of this bikini. Though dont expect great support for big busts. Almost gave it 4 stars because of the not so great support and the no machine wash thing. But those are pretty minor imo

It’s a gorgeous color and will look great when I’m tan. The bottoms are the type of fit I like, but they do cut in a little bit on my hips (not the fault of the suit, thanks childbirth). I’m pleasantly surprised as to how well the top fits. I’m a 36DDD and it covers them. Not super supportive without the straps, but I didn’t expect it to be. It covers the mom pooch, but a darker color would hide it even better if that’s something you’re concerned about. I ordered an XL and I’m between 180-185 lbs, approximate measurements: bust is 44”, waist is 34”, and hips are 45”

I really liked this bikini. Likely, I ordered size XL. Normally, I wear M (6-8 bottoms and 34DD top). Because of the top size I often order two suits in different size, but this is one is great. I am definitely taking it to my upcoming vacation trip. Usually, when the bikini bottoms are too big, they have too much fabric I the front, but these are perfectly adjustable and cheeky, so XL will fit anything M-L, no likely it will fit size 12 and above. The color is a bit shiny Paris blue, it is amazing for any skin color. There are small metallic details that are subtle but add the charm. The fabric is elastic, it hugs the curves well, looks the same wet or dry. The bra and the front of the bottoms are lines with very thin white fabric, which is sewn so that it won’t peek out, the seam is a broad overlock of blue. There are removable pads. I never use those, but can say these are good, they follow the shape of the bra closely. Overall, I recommend this bikini, it is pretty, classic and well made.

I like this tankini. The hibiscus flower pattern is exotic, and it is cut to be tight in the bust and flare out very wide over the hips. I am a somewhat pear-shaped 42C, and the bust on the extra large was just barely large enough for me, but there were inches and inches to spare in the hips (although the swimming trunks that accompany it were a bit tight on me). What really surprised me is that this suit is not hemmed at the bottom—it is just cut off! I don’t know if mine was somehow unfinished or if it was intended to be made that way. It is not frayed in any way, so perhaps it will be fine, but I am tempted to take out my sewing machine and hem it! I’m giving it 4 stars rather than 5 because of the lack of a hem. Apart from that, it’s a nice swimsuit and one that will work especially well for anyone who is pear-shaped or even pregnant.

I’m 5’2”, ~170lbs, wear an 8-10 range in pants and dresses and a 34D if I put on a real bra. I’ve got a pretty good sized butt and a lower stomach to counter it. Because of this, and the fact that I simply stopped reading size charts and went with review trends from people that look anything remotely like my shape, I got an XL. The whole suit is a great material and I really like the deep green. The clasp is just the metal hook that slides into a loop on the other side of the upper band. The bottoms fit me perfectly — I love the ruching on the back and the v-shape / fold in the front. The top is just too big. I loved it so much I thought I could just throw in a stitch to tighten the lower band (and be okay opting for full coverage in place some titty peak-a-boo), but then I started to feel like if I wanted the actual top to be secure, I’d need the strap to be tighter (as pictured) and even more so if I wanted some boob action. Great suit that only lost a star because I’m convincing myself that my body is no longer the problem, it’s the *clothing* that doesn’t fit *me* right. If the top was adjustable or you could buy the pieces in two sizes, I’d be all over this. I hope this helps someone who has had a tumultuous time shopping for swimmies. I hope you find joy in whatever swimsuit you end up in, because you deserve it.

Great bathing suit! It has a beautiful style for the lucky curvy ones among us and can also help slim one down (especially in the abdomen area) for the chunkier ones among us. It has modest lines for more coverage than most bathing suits. That suits me fine as I'm no bathing beauty, LOL! The material is somewhat thick, which makes it durable and helps hold in imperfections. It has interior bra padding which I believe can be removed if desired. I want comfort in a bathing suit, and it delivers. I found the suit comfortable without any pulling around the legs, crotch, or shoulder areas. This bathing suit features a small silver clasp at the upper back to join two small straps. It's a bit challenging to connect and open the clasp because of its design and also how it's positioned. SIZE SUGGESTION: I ordered an XL based on the size chart. I am normally a Medium or sometimes a Large. I wasn't sure what fit I'd get by following the size chart; it turned out to be a PERFECT choice. I'm 5'4-1/2" with a little chunkiness around hips and abdomen at the time of purchase; the XL torso fit great as well as the bust area. So, the size chart measurements were true to size for me. That means I ordered 1-2 sizes larger than my usual. This suit is not made for someone with plus-size measurements as I ordered the largest size available. It would be a nice suit for plus sizes so perhaps one day it will be offered in additional sizes. WASHING: The tag recommends handwashing this nylon/Spandex suit and line drying it. This is a quality bathing suit and will delight its wearer for a long time with the right care. I hope my information is .

Great, great, great tummy control bathing suit!I do have mixed emotions about this bathing suit. I’ll tell you why in a second. But first, for your perspective, I am a 40 y/o mother of three. My height is 5’7” and I weigh 186 lbs. My bra size is 40B. I am normally a size 12 or large. In the sizing guide for this bathing suit a size 12 is an XL, so I ordered an XL. The bathing suit fits like a glove.This bathing suit is really, really good quality. The material is strong, thick enough to control my main problem area, my tummy. Really amazing tummy control. Loved that from the get-go. The rushed tummy area is a miracle maker.The boobage area is fantastic. The cups help those like us who weren’t too blessed in that department and the cleavage is sexy, yet conservative. The straps are fantastic. They are adjustable and feel really comfortable. The cut of the bathing suit is not too low, but not too high. And the strings are adorable. It’s a bit cheeky in the butt area, but again, conservative enough. I think you can modify that as you wish with the strings. If you want them higher or lower. I’ll have to play with that.The back has a clasp as the closure, and I love that. It really is so comfortable. I can’t stand halter tops because they hurt my neck, so this bathing suit is everything I love.Here is where my problem lies: the cutout design in the back. As gorgeous as it is on super models and super athletic and thin women, I feel that for the average women with their beautiful mom bods, this cutout sits right where the rolls are the strongest. I am sooooo bummed out about that. I absolutely love this bathing suit, and my back rolls just are too exposed.I may or may not wear this bathing suit. I guess I have to gain the confidence to wear it and not care about the back rolls. Other than that, this bathing suit is a home run. Absolutely perfect.

Shipping - The dress arrived in a timely manner - within two days with Prime. Not stuffed in the mailbox this time - you’re finally getting it Amazon.Packaging - The dress was well packaged in Amazon’s bubble padded soft envelope. It came in it’s own plastic, as well.Quality - The product itself is typical swimwear quality, perhaps slightly less.Fit - I’m 5”0” and 155 lbs - ugh! I know, it’s too much. I’m usually a 12/14 in clothes if I still want to breathe while wearing them. Size medium in most clothes don't fit well any more. It seems too tight and makes me feel self-conscious. I usually spend the entire time pulling fabrics away from my body when I wear something that’s a medium. Large seems to be the best fit overall. So, in this case, I ordered a large. However, the swimdress did not fit that well. It was loose all over. I like to think that I lost some weight from when the dress was ordered and when it arrived, but that’s not likely. So, if I had to re-order, I would choose a medium(?). I’m just afraid it might have been too tight and I’d be back trying to pull fabrics away from the body. But, then again, maybe not because the flare on the dress could take care of that for me. But, would I have felt like I could breathe? I didn’t want to spend money on something that would make me self-conscious and uncomfortable, so I opted for the large. No regrets here.Style - The style is amazing. It’s just what I wanted. Something that doesn’t stick to your body and show every little bit of fat that you work so hard to hide every other moment of your life. Something that flatters your figure. Something that covers up the hip area so you can maintain some dignity at the pool/beach. Love it! Would definitely buy again.

Reference: 5’9 170lbs and size XL Likes: 1. Longer length shorts are modest; they are also very comfortable. No worries about your cheeks hanging out or awkward grabbing at upper thighs. 2. Built in bra is comfortable and holds the breasts in place. 3. Adjustable length top (drawstrings); makes it customizable if you have a longer or shorter torso. 4. Overall comfort—the suit it very comfortable to wear and I do feel confident in it. Dislikes: 1. Built-in PANTY creates lines where it digs into upper thighs and potentially across the cheeks. I think that these could reasonably be cut out of the suit though… 2. Waist line on shorts—it’s a strange construction with a weird hen. It sticks out through the shirt and shows if you belly sticks out in the slightest. I wish it was more tummy-flattering and smoothing, especially with my c-section pooch. 3. Print/pattern is a little cheesy. I picked the navy blue suit with diagonal stripe pattern on it. When it arrived, I saw that it was a silly, galaxy pattern with shooting stars—it’s just a bit cheap looking, which the picture on Amazon doesn’t really reflect. Overall impression: Yes, I would buy this suit again—for the price point and the functionality, I am happy with it.

Can a swimsuit be a Godsend? If so, this is it for me. I was shopping for something with underwire that would also keep “the girls” in place while I roughhouse with my little boys in the pool, but that would also cover my belly up to at least my belly button. This suit doesn’t have underwire (see the pics) but the combination of the crisscross pieces over the bust coupled with the wireless cups, really gives the girls a nice shape and holds them in place. I can’t emphasize the magic of the design enough. Normally my girls look like a mashed-up sports bra squished mess in a bathing suit, but not in this one. I really love the little peep in the front and the way the back straps cross and tie. The adjustable straps are amazing as well. The front of the suit gives pretty decent tummy control. The back covers a little more booty than I’d like, but I can move the sides in a little to show a little more cheek without it looking weird or too deliberate. Swimsuit shopping is normally a depressing nightmare for me, so I had to post this review immediately for anyone else looking for a bathing suit that seems to do it all. For reference, I am 5’10” and about 190lbs. I ordered a Blue Striped in XL, which is what Amazon recommended for me based on previous clothing purchases and it fits perfectly.

I’m 5’9”, 180lbs, 36G bra size. This was the first suit I’d tried on in this price range that actually felt like quality material that might last a while. The top is very form-fitted and “clingy,” not the type of tankini that will float around you in the water. It has thin adjustable straps (they cannot be disconnected/crossed). Despite the thinness of the straps, they are nicely seamed and quite comfortable. There is a pretty supportive shelf bralette with round, removable pads. I have a large, non-perky chest, and I was pleasantly surprised by how well my chest was covered and supported. I wouldn’t go for a jog, but a family game of water polo won’t risk freeing the girls. Top is long, can be ruched up to flatter a middle-aged waist without any danger of showing off middle-aged belly flesh. The bottom is high-waisted without being ridiculous, and offers solid medium coverage, has a bit of cheekiness without letting a cheek slip out easily. There is very little “bite” to the waistband (and I’m prone to muffin top), with comfortable seams. The color block pattern is fun and bold. I will own at least one or two more of these suits. Excellent value for the price!

Being pregnant, its been hard to find flattering clothing let alone swim wear and I had to leave a positive review for my new favorite swimsuit! Size fit: I am currently 7 months, 5’5, 185 pounds pregnant. My cup size has ballooned to 38 DDD. Chest: I feel well supported with the pads. They don’t seem to be removable and there is an underwire. Based on the reviews, I went a size up to reduce the risk of the boning poking me since I busty. For less busty women, this item fits true to size and I wouldn’t recommend any looser since the shoulder straps are not adjustable. Silhouette: Slides over my belly and creates a cute baby doll look. I appreciate being able pull the shorts lower and hiding problem areas. Design: Color is bold and hasn’t faded. Patterns are flattering if you are trying to hide a little belly. All in all, you are getting more for a typical swimsuit in this price range. You won’t be disappointed.

The app suggested XL would fit me best, and despite my leanings towards my usual size L, I went with the XL suggestion based on some reviews. I'd say if you're more well endowed up top, go for the larger size. For me, I feel like Large would have fit me well. I'm 5'6", 190 lbs, somewhat athletic build, still recovering from having twins, pear shaped body type. I'm also wearing a new discovery in the picture to pair with the swim suit, they're swim leggings (YES, THAT'S A THING! 🙌🏼), Capri style, absolutely brilliant if your legs suffer from weight gain and cellulite like mine do. I'm ashamed of my legs and refuse to be photographed in shorts or a swim suit because of them, so for our upcoming vacation I found these brilliant leggings to accompany my new suit. I haven't worn them in the pool yet, just reviewing quickly for the other recent mom's like me looking to find a nice covering swim suit that doesn't accentuate the excess tummy skin for the upcoming dreaded swim suit season. If you have fit legs, the skirt part will be the perfect length for you. If you're average build and hold yourself with proper posture (shoulders back, chin up, tummy in, hips back) then it really helps you look your best and allows for the lovely pleating shown in the product image. (Pics 2 & 3) I will say though that the "shorts" section starts really low, leaving about 2" of "crackage" (I'm calling that now as the newest trend term) visible through the white middle liner section, so I'm glad I have the leggings to go over that for the inevitable skirt floatage in the pool water. I had a similar style maternity swim suit and the skirt always floated as high as the water level went, when fully imursed, it would float up to the seem level, so I expect this will do the same. I have twin toddlers now though, so they'll be clinging to me assisting in keeping the skirt section down. Just something to keep in mind as you shop for this style swim suit, as that can be embarrassing and downright annoying if you're not expecting it. This style is best for beach days or lounging pool side, not so much for public swimming pool regular swimming and water fun. The white section also is not a slimming type of material but lends well to ladies with more mid section or maternity bellies. I hope this review helped, if so, please click the button. Thanks! And happy shopping!

Ok, so this is going to be the long review about my body type that I wish I had. I am bottom heavy so dresses that fit my hips well are usually way to big on my chest. See my measurements below. I got an XL and it fits my waist comfortably. It is too big on my chest but not too bad. I definitely should go a size or 2 up for my hips but then I would be swimming in fabric up top. The shorts are definitely very short, essentially underwear with no hope of preventing thigh chafing. If the skirt comes up you'll see a lot of crack since my butt pulls the top of the shorts lower than they are meant to be. The shorts are tight on my thighs as you can see but not so tight that it's uncomfortable. All in all, it's a cute suit, that fits decently, with what feels like quality material so I'm gonna keep it. Alright, alright, alright... This is a good one!!! I ordered this Aqua Eve Women High Waisted Bikini Set in an XL because I live in a swimsuit in the summer and like to change them like i change my daily clothes. This one is definitely one of my new favorites! For reference, I'm 5'5ish, 185ish, 38D and 30" waist... and I truly feel like this suit fit me like a glove! Here's what I like about it: •THE CUT! The cut of this suit is modest, fashion-forward and visually interesting. I feel like it is pretty flattering on my midsize body. Plus, it covers enough so that I can actually swim and play in the pool with the kids comfortably. The bow on the bottoms is fun, too. (It isn't super cheeky either.) •THE COLORS! These are colors that you just don't see on a suit very often. I love it! The rose pattern on the top is pretty and different. •WELL MADE! This suit is made of thick material that is structured, and stretchy, but you can't see any dimples underneath it. The cups in the top are removable-- which is what I always do, but to each their own. If you are expecting a suit with a ton of support up top, this isn't the one. But if you are wanting a suit that is super comfortable, easy to move around in, fun and different-- you should order it. I lightly recommend it!!!

I have been pregnant three times now, and I’ve gone through countless maternity bathing suits. Since I don’t want to spend tons of money on some thing that I will only wear for a shorter amount of time, I like to scan Amazon for the suits as opposed to designer brands. This one is the perfect one by far! The fabric isn’t super thick, but it’s ribbed and thick enough to feel like it’s holding you in, while also being very soft. I like how it doesn’t have a keyhole in the back, I like full upper back coverage. The straps are adjustable, which is a must have. The set overall is very nice and will leave you with extra room to grow. I am 5’4”, 30 weeks pregnant and about 200 pounds and got an XL. I could have easily gotten away with a large, but I wanted to leave room for the last 10 weeks where I know I will be growing more.

I’ve been looking for a bikini that was a tad skimpy but not a thong. We just moved back to Phoenix and have a pool and I plan on getting my tan on! Haha Price point was nice. Better than target prices or other websites that have a ton of high waisted bottoms. I’m so not into that style.. AT ALL! My husband hates the high waist trends that have been around too. Jeans, swim, everything. Yuck. I grew up in the low rise era haha! 04 ish time. I have 3 boys so I feel covered up but still sexy for my husband, vs frumpy, and able to show enough skin to work on my tan. My measurements: 38DD 33” waist 45” hip/size 12/ sometimes 10 dependent on brand. I’ve got a bubble butt and birthing hips lol! But also a curvy athletic build. The green is a nice soft sage/army green? Hard to describe but I love it and it was exactly what I was looking for! My eyes are green so I wanted something to help them pop as well as my tan while hanging out makeup-free by the pool all summer. :) I hand wash to keep color from fading. Very little soap… almost a spot wash. Hang dry as well. I’ve worn it about 10 times already and have been in and out of our clorinated pool a bunch and no fading. I’m careful not to sit on our cool decking or get up carefully so I don’t scratch up the material. Swimwear is prone to that… I saw other reviews but I think that’s just a given with swimwear. Be mindful. I also don’t rub up against my pool siding or sit on the seats or steps bc that it’s even more scratchy. Great price point to replace it when I wear this one down!! I love the scrunchy butt! Sexy but not too bad. I did have a tummy tuck after having 3 boys naturally! 9.9lbs, 10.9lbs and 10.2lbs all 22”. I grow big babies! :) hence my birthing hips lol! Back to what I was saying: this covers my TT scar! But I had an amazing Dr!! I’ve seen some gnarly Tt scars out there. I had some other bikinis last year and the year before that were thicker and others said were Tt safe, but I feel like they have too much coverage and aren’t as flattering. But they were still great! I just like this style of bottom better. Sexier. I just ordered the orange this AM too! Can’t wait! I highly recommend. Update: Got the orange one - same size and the top is a little smaller.. so it doesn’t look right on my 38DD.. pretty disappointed! So just an fyi.. however I’ll probably try another color… just beware of sizing… same brand/style and size but wasn’t correct in the right size… :( and without cups it’s seethru when wet- which doesn’t fly with my 3 little boys and their friends having pool time! 😬😬

Now I know I don't have too much bump yet, but I just turned 20 weeks and am starting to show a little. I've also gained weight, of course, and my legs are swollen from an 11hr day at work standing yesterday, so with that all in mind - here's my pics and review. I'm expecting a June baby, so I wanted a swimsuit at the ready. I loved the color options and had a hard time choosing, but I ended up going with the teal. The peekaboo belly is really cute, so are the scalloped edges and button details. The ruching makes me feel like I might even be able to use it postpartum if I want to swim and want to hide my belly until I lose what I put on. I am 5'9" and usually a 16/xl so I got the xl and find it true to size. The cut isn't too narrow at the crotch or on the cheeks so I appreciate the relatively modest coverage. I also really like that the fabric is thick and feels like a good quality swimsuit - not thin or see-through at all. It comes with removable cup liners and the shoulder straps are adjustable. For me at the moment, I felt like it could be more flattering, but maybe I'll like the cut more as it fits my growing bump. I would recommend it if you're looking for a quality suit with modest coverage.

Sharing the sage green color since there weren’t many examples of customer photos in green. It took longer to come in than the white one would have. The dress is definitely shapeless but I would wear it with just a few buttons clasped. This makes a great ‘fit when heading to the pool or short walk to the beach. You could probably even change out of your swim suit under here it’s so spacious. I do think it’s very over priced at almost $40. It’s not see-through, but could use steaming to get the wrinkles out. I am keeping this since I leave for my trip in less than a week. For reference I’m 5 ft 1 in tall, 211 lbs. 40D bust and it’s not tight in the back/arms if you’re only wearing your swim suit or a tank under it. It’s nice and thick but wrinkles terribly and whats the point of a dress that doesn’t close- can’t even change out of your swimsuit underneath.

Note: the top is a separate item I am reviewing that just happens to match perfectly with this swim bottom.To start, the swim bottom fabric is comfortable, there's no itchy or scratchy feeling from it anywhere. However, the bottoms run small. How small? That would depend on the person, but in the photos, my almost 20 year old daughter who is an athlete and in fairly good shape is wearing a size XL. She never wears an XL! She's a medium in quality made apparel and a lot of times a large in cheaper Chinese made apparel. If that helps give you any idea for sizing?! These also have the panty sewn inside and it was comfortable as well. After the small sizing, I'd say the biggest thing I think people might have a problem with is it bunching up in front. Especially if you don't have the smooth, flat athletic body type that allows this swim bottom to lie flat in front and show off the side ruched look. For everyone else who either has a tummy pooch, rolls, muffin top, or a keg (vs. a 6 pack), this ruched look will only (possibly) accentuate what you have. And because the elastic band around the top is thin and not a nice wide band for those of us needing a little support or control, it too will only accentuate any rolls you have in that area (and because it runs small). If you're completely confident in yourself and don't mind what shows and how, good for you and go for it. But for the rest of us, maybe look at other bottoms. For this 55 year old aging athlete, I've tried several other styles like this before, but they had wider waistbands and for some reason were more flattering on than this bottom was on me.

Are you a tall or busty girl, looking for a great basic swimsuit, on a budget, and are tired of crying in dressing rooms? (Ok I guess no one is crying in dressing rooms these days...) What a find. NOTE: I am a member of the Amazon Vine Program. I try out new products, and then provide an honest, unbiased review (the good, the bad, the ugly, and the questionable), based on my personal impressions and experience. I am not paid for my reviews. I also have a very large bustline (DD), and was worried about how well this style would support "the girls", but my concerns were unwarranted. Although there is no underwire, there's something about the criss-cross style and the elastic surrounding the front cutout that provides plenty of modest coverage and support that was beyond my expectations. Considering that I often spend $100-$200 to buy a swimsuit that supposedly performs "miracles" for my shape, I think this suit is an excellent value at less than $30 (at the time of my review), based on the quality of the item I received. Highly recommend!

UPDATE: I broke down and bought a better quality swimsuit (Sunsets) and am sending the suit I thought I would keep, back. There is a huge difference in quality, and I thought why on earth would I pay someone to tack down the dress and tighten up the straps? The better quality swimsuit beats this one any day. I bought this suit specifically because the description said it had underwire and the padding could be removed. Neither is true. There is a side wire, which is very but no true underwire. Nor does it come out, which I was ok with. I’m not ok with the padding because it does not come out! There is a slit in the bra as if it might be removable, however it is sewn in. The girls really don’t need that extra help. I would also benefit from adjustable straps. I bought a 2nd suit that I am going to keep that I will have to have altered to pull up the straps. As I was expecting, the part that connects the “shorts” to the suit is white and there is zero control; for me that was ok because the skirt did a good job covering up my pudge. The problem I foresee, especially with the black, is when I’m swimming and the skirt floats up, that’s a sight I don’t want anyone else to see! I am going to have the seamstress tack it to either side of the short portion to hold it down. 36DD(sometimes 3rd D) 165lbs and 5’6”. The other suit I bought was the Prussian blue color, which turned out to be more of an darkish teal green. I was sad about that, but I’m keeping it 1) because it was cheap and 2) I look halfway decent in it even though my boobs are front and center. Good luck. Don’t forget… you get what you paid for! Not terrible for sure but not quite what I wanted.

This isn't the best swim top I own but it definitely isn't the worst. For the record, this is advertised as a plus sized swimsuit top and I don't particularly consider myself plus sized. BUT I am very busty. My overbust measurement is 41.5" and my underbust is 34", that said I wear a US 34H bra. For this reason alone, I considered that this may be a potentially decent fit and proceeded to order. I ordered the smallest size available and again, it's very nice. The fabric is soft, color is vibrant, is fully lined and has adjustable shoulder straps and a back strap. While the suit is very well made, there are a few issues I had. I understand the purpose of the foam inserts in the cup, but for a plus sized top, they were quite small. I fully believe this is meant for someone who may be a bit bigger around in the band and smaller in the cup. I found the band to be a bit loose on me and the space allotted to cover the bust just didn't seem like enough fabric. For those reasons, I had to cup the band underneath the girls to prevent underboob. The back, while partly adjustable, was difficult to hook. I had to do the "hook in front and twist around"method. I'll primarily be wearing this as support beneath my rash guard so that should be okay but just know that you definitely will want to pay attention to the size chart. I'm not saying it needs underwire but perhaps larger cups. I've seen swimsuits that have the foam support pads sewn into the suit and they make a world of a difference. A thicker support band would also be . For bras, the majority of the support comes from the band, straps and underwire or molded cups help work support and shaping. I think a wider band would add more support and security. Lastly, making the back band fully adjustable with either a hook and eye closure with multiple rows or have the slide hook closure it has now but allow for the full back to be tightened. Overall I think this is great for plus sized ladies who may have up to a DDD cup but any larger than that and you're in for some trouble.

I’ll talk about the bottoms first and then the top. I’m not sure if it shows up or not but, for reference, I am 5’2” and I weigh about 110 pounds. I bought the XS because I’d rather have tight bikini bottoms over loose bottoms for swimming. The bottoms are stretchy, so I’m glad I sized down. I don’t think they will come down in the water, but if I would have gone with a small I feel like they would because of how much stretch is there. Overall, the bottoms are perfect. Wouldn’t change a thing about them. As for the top, it’s a love/hate relationship. I wear a size 34b and the xs is tolerable, but its definitely snug around me. I like the look of the top, it’s cute overall. However, the amount of fabric they give you I can definitely see a possible nip slip happening. The coverage is more for the outer part of your boob than the inner if you get what I’m saying, so depending on where your nip sits, there’s a good chance it will make an appearance. I’m trying to consider my options here. I’ve thought about using my sewing skills to add a little more fabric so that it’s not a concern. But I’m not sure yet. Overall, I like more about this suit than hate, so I will be keeping it. For the price, I feel like it’s a cute suit and I can work around the one thing I don’t like and will possibly fix myself. I think this suit is worth taking a chance on and if you don’t like certain things with sizing just return for a smaller or bigger size.

To start off, I just want to say I am 12 weeks pregnant, 5'4", 176 lbs, 36B bra size pre-pregnancy (already need new bras), 34" waist, 44" hips, and I ordered a 2XL in the color Cyan. I read A LOT of reviews to figure out if I wanted this swimsuit and if it would be ideal for pregnant women. When I received it and tried it on, I wasn't sure if it'd fit, but it does! For the most part anyway. The shorts are just a little bit tight but not bad enough where I have to return it...it's still comfortable. Also this suit is probably made for bustier women, but I fit in there fine without cleavage showing. Since I'll be growing in that area as well due to pregnancy, it doesn't bother me. It has underwire, is padded, and even has a push up that you can take out if you want to! I'll be leaving mine in as it's more comfortable for me. The white spandex underneath the swimsuit for the belly is also very stretchable so I am sure there will be plenty of room for my growing bump in the summer months. The skirt comes to mid thigh for me. The swimsuit is overall very cute, flattering, and comfortable. Makes me feel confident enough to go swim! It also came with a cute pearl bracelet! It is too small for my wrist, but my little sister will love it. I will be posting photos along with my review so you can see what it looks like on me. My bathroom lighting sucks and the pics are a bit blurry but I will post many in different angles. The color Cyan is 100% like the picture in real life as on Amazon, even though it looks otherwise in my photos. On another note, I received two swimsuits when I ordered and paid for only one. I am sending the other one back tomorrow. The return shipping was free on my part. All I have to do is print the label and stick it on the box and bring it to the post office. It was overall very easy and was pleased with the return service I was provided.

This review is for the teal floral in size xl (10-12). I am 5'4, bust size 36C. 160lbs. My average swimsuit size is a 14-16. I am one of those people who never order clothes online, especially swimsuits. However, I am going on a cruise and I wanted to find a swimdress that was flattering but also had a skirt that covered my backside and my upper thighs. After a LOT of research I decided on Cocopear. I read that others had said to order a size smaller than you are used to but I couldn't fathom that I could fit in a 10-12 so I ordered a xl (10-12) and a xxl (14-16). The xxl fit but didn't fit snug and the cups were too big. The xl fit snug and held in my tummy plus the cups fit much better. The colors on my monitor show the suit to be a bluer teal than it really is in real life. . In one of the pictures you can see both the suits side by side and they are two different shades. The larger one is more of a pine green with blue undertones.Pro- Suit in constructed well and is a heavier fabric than some store bought suit. The straps although not adjustable are soft on the shoulders. It has attached shorts and are comfortable on the legs. The bra cups have removable padding and are underwired.Con- The only thing I don't care for is the bra cups are stiff. The first thing I thought of was, that from the inside it looks like a coconut bra. Perhaps after washing they will soften up. That being said that is enough to make me not like this suit. In fact I may purchase more. I have see other suits that look like Cocopear but the ratings on Cocopear are better and after purchasing Cocopear, I won't try the others.

The bottoms are white and seem see-through. I haven't been in the water with this yet! I have a big bum, so the back of the dress rides up a little for me. Please see all photos, don't mind my typical girl bathroom filled with all my girl stuff! I took a picture of how the bra stands up all on their own! I would definitely buy this again if they had prettier pink floral designs. I wish this dress was in pink, because I don't know how the white will do in the water!

First of all I haven't put on a swimsuit in over 10 years due to weight gain and a hail storm that severely damaged my backside, Lol. I feel comfortable and almost cute in this one!I am very impressed with the material. It's of a very nice weight and quality. I went by the measurements and based on my waist and hip measumemts I ordered a XXL. I'm a C cup and the top is a little loose but not much. It is looser through the torso than I expected but very comfortable. Not constraining at all. It doesn't really "hold me in" but the flattering skirt hides all that. I could have probably got a XL but feel like it would have been too tight in the bottom and thighs. The boy shorts bottom could come up a bit higher, as some have stated. If your bottom is larger, the top of it is not really covered completely. The white material connecting the shorts to the top is thinner and more sheer. I may wear undies under it as another reviewer said. I am 5'2" and 176lbs. I have a short torso so this comes way down on my thighs but I don't see that as a bad thing. Again, the hail storm! I'm really happy and impressed with this purchase.

But, I also have zero self love so DON'T BE LIKE ME AND GO LOVE YOURSELF YOU HOT QUEEN. YOU WILL LOOK AMAZING, like all these other hot ladies wearing these suits <3 The suit itself is SUPER COMFY. The top doesn't poke, it holds things in place well, and the straps are actually removable so you can do a cross back if that supports your boobs better. The bottoms are high waisted which I usually hate, but these were not uncomfortable at all. If you have confidence and want a comfy suit, this is 100% the one. It's cute, comfy, and made well.

I absolutely love this suit! The only thing I would change is how deep the V is on the chest, only by a smidge. The coverage is great and the quality is fantastic! For reference I am 5’6” 240lbs and carry weight in my stomach and legs.

This review is for the Grace Karin Swimsuit with tummy control and ruffle v neck, xx-large, haze blue. My measurements are 38DD, 41” waist, 51” hips which means that my bikini top is normally an XL (sometimes a large) and my bottom is definitely a XXL. I’ve always worn bikini separates, in part to accommodate the different sizes. But I recently began a quest to find a cute and comfortable one-piece suit. This suit is gorgeous. The haze blue is a soft smokey color. The ruffled sleeve flows nicely and isn’t too poofy. The neck is a crisscross V that covers my chest comfortably without being high cut; I have no fear of falling out of the top. It has removable cups. The suit is double lined everywhere except for in the back and butt. There’s a slight ruching detail underneath the bust, which I like. I didn’t feel like this had tummy control but rather just that the fabric over the stomach was more substantial to help things look a little more smooth. That being said, I could see a little bit of an outline of the void that is my belly button through the suit when dry. It has very full backside coverage and the hips are low like a brief cut. I tried hiking up the bottom of the suit at the hips a little bit (to elongate my not-long legs) but the style didn’t allow for that. I love so much about this suit and want the fit to be perfect but *for me* it’s not. My bust is a little bit smaller (38DD, 43”) than the 44-47” listed in the size chart but it didn’t feel excessively roomy. But the bottom of the bust hit too low for me. Did I mention that I’ve breastfed 5 babies? I want that bottom support to be high. I’ve included a picture where I’m pointing to where I’d like the underbust band to hit. Overall this suit is gorgeous and well made- it doesn’t feel cheap at all. I think it’s priced very well for the quality and extra details that it has.

This swimsuit is adorable. Modest & covers everything it should, while still being stylish. Very comfortable fit. I ordered a size up from what I typically wear, as most clothing from the zon tends to run small. I wear XL but chose a 2X. I feel like it fits well on the bottom, but just a little big in the bust & stomach. I have a pronounced 🍑 & usually have to size up my swimsuit bottoms anyway & why I usually wear a 2 piece. My true size would have been fine. It doesn't ride up & stays in place. The skirt can be worn down or tied on the side, for a more fun look, without exposing too much. This does have a padded bra with an eleastic band for support. The pads can be removed from tiny pockets on the inside. I got this for an upcoming family vacation with inlaws & extended family. I wanted something that had full coverage but didn't make me feel older than I am or like I'm wearing an oversized trashbag 😆. This was the perfect choice. Made from quality thick material. Doesn't feel cheap. Ruching in the middle hides my mom pooch. Has tummy flattening properties but not so much it feels like I'm squeezing into shapeware. I only removed a star because for whatever reason the bust makes the girls look flat. I'm a C cup & usually have to find swimsuits that keep them tucked in, whereas this is just the opposite. Even with the pads, its like they're pushed down by the bra, instead of being supported & cupped. With the deep V neck, it looks weird not to have even the smallest amount of cleveage. Could just be because I sized up or maybe it's meant to be that way for modesty. Overall, I still think this is a great swimsuit. It's so comfortable that I imagine this will be my go-to suit for the summer.

I'm a 29yo mom of 3, I'm 5'8" and I fluctuate between 195-200lbs. 5’2 145-ish lbs. boob size at 36-38 DD-ish. I Ordered a large. This must be one of the most flattering 2 pieces for a short stout aging body changing (doing whatever it wants) & distributing weight all over the place. I have that “middle age spread” pretty bad. Clearly I’ll never have an attractive body. Too much abuse and repeated weight gain & loss over the years. My stomach is all jacked up from having kids. I lost my old hourglass shape that was always distinguished no matter how large or small I was (except when pregnant). I got saddle bags all day long. My upper arms are yuge. But even though I don’t have an attractive body, I will at least look presentable in a two piece. The lower portion comes up enough to hide my rumply, crepey lower abdomen. The top adds support, lift, and substance to visually supplement some needed volume & proportion in the upper body. It is the last stretch of winter in the northeast USA, the WORST time for me as I’m ghastly white and haven’t been outside burning calories in MONTHS but I have to go. Even the ties in the back (I wish I could take a picture)...If you have that backfat fold like kinda under your arms , if you tie the top it in the back just so...you can disguise it...or at least take some of focus off of its appearance. I have this trip to a place where it’s warm year round and most of the women there will probably have fit & athletic bodies because the world didn’t stop and leave them indoors like a reclusive for almost six months, so with this swimsuit, I am less mortified to go. I have not worn a 2 piece in probably over 10 years. Whoever thought up this design is a genius. Also, I may have been able to wear a medium because when I tie the ties, and it feels like I’m tieing all the way it can possibly go. There’s a chance a medium may even look more flattering than this, Maybe not. I am not sure. It’s always a little risky I think to try the smaller possibilities. I could only know for sure if I was able to try them both on. So if you are my size or a little smaller than me... know that you might be borderline the small side of a large also... maybe even a medium.

My build: I am boxy with a short torso 5’5 ft and am currently 130 lbs, with a solid/athletic build. I’m small chested: A 34 Aish-Bish, post kids. I’m still trying to learn my new body after children. I don’t want to knock this suit too much because some of my biggest problems really may just be unrealistic personal expectations and my specific build. I’m at a year and a half (ok, almost 2 years) out from having had my second bambino. Following that pregnancy, I have a delicious leftover herniated belly button, loose skin, 10 lbs that refuse to come off, a few stretch marks, and a mama bear crease. So ordering this suit was an attempt to basically have a glorified one piece that covered all that hot mess, maintained some modesty, and helped me stop looking like I was on my way to a swim meet in my mom-speedo. I typically wear a small now with some M and some XS depending on how the clothes run. I was a pretty solid size 0-2 XS and 32 A, before kids, so if you’re built similarly and, like me, are still struggling to figure out your new sizes and body after kiddos, this may help. I ordered the small. The top is big. My nibblets definitely do not fit the cups, but it’s made in such a way that no one would know, and it doesn’t gape open. The bottoms are my biggest issue (with my build). I feel like I may need medium bottoms. They’re a bit snug and are uncomfortably tight but are still workable-ish. But if anything, I feel like I need an XS top. So I’m a little perplexed on returning or not. Perhaps this will help my fellow boxy, small chested sisters. If I could go back, I probably wouldn’t have ordered this suit, and checked out one in store where I could mix sizes. I could see how with the right build, this could be a really great suit for some though 😊 Also, the material is not too thin. Good luck in deciding!

Let me start by saying WOW! I love it! I was worried about several things when buying a bathing suit online 1. Would my boobs be flopping allover the place? I am a 34F 2. Would the bottoms actually be high enough on my torso to be over my belly button and cover my loose skin from having gigantic twins? 3. Would the straps around the back fall in just the right place to accentuate my back fat? Short answers: -Amazing breast support with structured cups and adjustable back. (If your boobs are down to your belly button a bathing suit won’t support them period, expecting that isn’t fair) that being said I accept my sag. -Bottom is high enough that it covers exactly what I want covered (and my saggy skin is way higher up than other moms because I had two seven lb babies at one time, stretched me to the max!). - The back straps fall in the perfect place to HIDE my back rolls!!!!! Full butt coverage. I would say the material is a 6-7 on a scale of 10 but for the price it exceeds expectations. For reference I’m 5’3”, 157lbs, size 8-10 pants, size L underwear and pants , I’ve had three children. I am a 40” chest, 34 waist, 42.5” hip. I read the reviews and size chart and decided to go with the XL. I’ve never been and XL in anything but I am very happy with the sizing. It’s not tight so I don’t look like exploding biscuits. I think once wet it may be semi loose but not so much that I would have sized down. I’ve never felt sexy again after having kids. This suit helps me feel sexier and more comfortable walking around in a bathing suit.

I ordered this bikini as soon as I saw “size 2-10” on the small-medium. I was intrigued and really hopeful that I had found what was sure to be the most comfortable and perfect fit on the market. I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 Jean and small or medium bikinis. I have extra wide hips and struggle to find swimsuit bottoms that don’t ride up since my booty is upside-down heart shaped. It is fascinating how much stretch this bikini has. Truly! It looks teeny tiny but stretches to mold around my body, neither too tight nor loose. The fabric is kind of thick for a swimsuit. There is very little bust support in the top so I would not recommend for a game of sand volleyball. The bottoms actually stay in place very well! I usually can’t stand cheeky bottoms because they ride up my bum but I did some laps around my bedroom and they stayed. I really like that the stretch allows the sides of the bottoms to be high or low on the hips. I love it. This bikini is light coverage with big comfort. Perfect for a day of lounging or light water use. Not the most flattering top for my chest because it does give the pinched boob look due to no boning, lift, or padding. But again, super comfortable, like wearing a thin bralette.

I'm 5/4" and 155 lbs, and I usually wear a size Medium top and Medium (or occasionally Large) on the bottom. I went with Amazon's recommendation to get a Large in this swimsuit, but it's still too small. I extended the adjustable straps all the way and removed the bra pads, but it's still so tight that my back bulges out over the top. Because of the tightness, it's difficult to pull on and take off. On the plus side, this is a cute suit with a retro feeling. It isn't cut too high in the legs or too low at the top, which I appreciate. The fabric is very nice, soft with a ribbed look on the outside and smooth inside. There's a safety seal over the crotch area that is easily removed. I washed it in cool water on gentle cycle and line dried it, and it came out just fine. It might fit me just fine if I happen to lose five pounds, but I can't comfortably wear it now.

(Before I start, I’m 5ft 4, 138lbs, Size Medium, Size 9, 29 waist of this helps at all?) The first 2 photos is what the problem is for most people — the extra material at the crotch region. They’re perfect, but I roll may cuffs by habit anyways, so, that’s where the 4th photo kicks in. I like the cuff look better on me. The last photo is another angle focusing on the material in that region. I am planning to buy another in a small (same color) and perhaps that will be better so it won’t have the stretchy pouch there. I’ve read some reviews where alot of women are saying these aren’t stretchy? Perhaps it differs for color/style? Because these are STRETCHY. Best way to describe these overalls: The material for Jeggings. They look like Jean material but stretchy asf. Are these worth $40+? No. I wish they was alittle cheaper. Luckily for me, a flash sale popped up and I got them less than $40.

(lost a star for color difference) I'm not big on skinny jeans, but I came across these and thought I'd give them a try. (sorry for the blurry mirror) For reference I’m 5’4” 135lbs and a 31 year old mom. I ordered these first in size 4/27 (my normal Levi’s size) but they were too big. I re-ordered in size 2/26 and they fit perfectly. I am normally not a size 2/26 so they ran large on me. They are extremely flattering on my pear shape. NO gap in the back of the waist! I have a thick bottom half (and short legs) and I don’t like to wear short shorts. These fit really nice! Nice mid to high rise on them. You can see in my picture it comes to about my belly button. You don’t have to worry about bending over and showing plumbers butt. The dark wash is really nice and classy looking. The pocket placement is great. They are overall extremely flattering. A little bit of stretch, but not too much. Pictures taken when the shorts were new but they’ve been washed and worn since with no issues to the actual shorts. My only issue was with the denim wash. I did have to use a color catcher while washing. It still dyed one of my white hand towels light blue! Be careful of color transfer with the dark wash! The only thing I wish was the bottom leg holes of the shorts was a tiny bit smaller so it hugged my knees a little tighter. I’m just picky! The bottom is not sewn into place so you have to roll the shorts when you get them out of the dryer. They are really flattering on the backside and I definitely recommend them for pear shapes like me. I love not having to pull shorts down from riding up when I walk. That is why I like bike shorts and Bermuda shorts. If you have big thighs you know what I mean. I think curvy girls in particular will love these. The quality on mine is very good! I am definitely going to stock up and order a bunch for summer. I wish they had more colors in my size. Hopefully this review was to someone out there!

2023 UPDATE - when I say that I made these nearly an everyday staple for the past 2 years, I'm not joking. I wore these almost everyday, washed and dried them in the dryer endlessly. I finally purchased another pair (identical size and color as previously) and I am AMAZED at how well these held up! The wash slightly faded and there is one small spot where a thread loop (decorative only) came out of one back pocket.. that's IT. The fit hasn't shrunk in the slightest. So DO NOT HESITATE to buy these extremely high quality for the price and comfy jeggings!!! I'm always looking for inexpensive comfy jeggings for around the house when I want to feel like I'm still in pajamas but not necessarily *look* like I'm wearing pajamas. Grabbed these in a size Small. FIT I would say these run small. Not insanely small, but in particular the elastic waistband is too small to be comfortable. I'm a 28" waist and generally order everything in XS but this waistband is really tight on my waist. Other than the waistband I like the fit of these leggings. I am 5'3 and they hit just below my ankle which I prefer. QUALITY These are super stretchy but still look like authentic Jean fabric and stitching so it looks good and feels easy to move around in. LOOK/STYLE I like the wash of these jeggings, they're exactly as I hoped for. I don't think that the overly small waistband makes these flattering on me so I will only be wearing these with longer shirts that cover most of that area. The stitching and look of the fabric is an authentic Jean look and not cheap looking at all. FINAL THOUGHTS I do think these run small and if you dry clothes in the dryer like I do, I expect these will eventually shrink and become even smaller. I recommend sizing up at least one size, maybe 2 if you really want to be comfy. I would likely purchase more of these in more colors and medium size next time

Color & Transparency: Color is as pictured, slightly transparent but wearably so- i have tattoos on my legs and bum and if pulled tight you can see them, but for normal wear they’re just fine. A nice fitting pair of jeans, that are comfy are an investment for me. I'm all about quality, fit and comfort with jeans. I'll pay a higher price if need be. I'm somewhat hard to fit and picky when it comes to jeans. These were cute and the reviews were convincing. I'm a true size 4 in jeans and pants. 27 inch waist. I have narrow hips and pants usually creep down on me and I have to crank them as the day goes on, if I don't have a skinny belt on. These have an elastic band, and they feel like the'll stay "put." LOL Pay close attention when you read their sizing chart. Make sure your'e reading the correct chart for sizing. Either skinnys or boot cut, because both charts are different sizing. The first pair I ordered was a size 2 28' waist. Too small! Returned those and ordered a size 4 29" waist and those fit. I received the second pair promptly. I ordered 32" inseam. So, the manufacturer tag is marked 4L/waist 27 L32. Again, this varies from the size chart listed on the add. Other than the confusing sizing, they are cute,fit nice and comfy. The waist is elastic but isn't "overly" stretchy though. Somewhat light weight compared to other jeans, only moderate amount of stretch. Hopefully they won't get too baggy and stretched out the longer the wear time.

For reference: I'm 5'5" and weigh roughly 155lbs. I normally wear M on top, L on bottom. This is a size L and I may have been fine with a M.

Acabo de recibirlos. Decidí comprar este color porque se ve blanco en las fotos, aunque dice que es lila, así que me esperaba que no fuera del mismo color de la imagen ; además nadie había hecho una reseña para este color. Es cómodo y definitivamente elástico pero no esperes una elasticidad de leggins, estos son JEANS! Pero definitivamente se estiran más que los típicos Jeans que tienen botonadura, pero que también tienen cierta elasticidad (ver foto) No estaba posando en las fotos y me veo más gorda de lo que estoy dispuesta a admitir, pero, no se ven alagadores. Los voy a conservar porque puedo arreglar lo holgado de las piernas. Lo único bueno que puedo decir, es que de la cintura me queda perfecto, pero la cintura no estira casi nada, así que me costó mucho que pasaran por mis caderas y eso que no tengo caderas anchas ni trasero tan grande. Mis medidas (inch): Cintura: 35.5" Caderas: 41.5 Mi altura es 4ft 9ich (Sí, soy casi un pigmeo lol) Así que me quedan largos como se puede ver en las fotos, pero tiene arreglo.

According to the size chart measurements I decided that a size 16 would be the right one for me. These actually have a lot of stretch in them, so I maybe could have gone for a 14 instead as I do occasionally have to hitch these pants up again, lol. Not the fault of the pants though! They are made of 71% cotton, as stated on the product page and on the tag inside the jeans, so they may shrink when I wash and dry them, and that will help with the slipping down. They have four functioning pockets, two front with flaps that snap closed and two back pockets without flaps. They are somewhat short, so that makes them great for my height (5' 4") and under, I think. Even on me, they just barely get to my ankles. The material is sort of thick, so I think they will be nicely warm in the winter. I have worn them a couple times with temperatures around 70 F and usually I am happy to get home and put something cooler on. I love the color of them and the comfy fit. With all the stretch they have, there is no binding or pinching, no matter what direction you can contort your legs into. They are well made and all around just a nice pair of jeans, especially for shorter people. I definitely can recommend this pair of jeans. Comfort and fit are great with them.

Thanks Wallflower for catering to the real woman, and not just the teenie bopper stick figure... definitely one extra satisfied customer here!

As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

As w/ seemingly many others, I bought my first pairs @ CVS (got 3). I don’t normally shop for clothes there, but they were a serendipitous discovery, and having a hard-to-fit body where I don’t often find items that I can wear off-the-rack (I’m an endomorph, or “apple” type, where my waist is short & thick), I wanted to look for more pairs, solids specifically (black, heather grey & indigo w/ gold threading), to have as wardrobe basics. I was THRILLED to find them on Amazon (and they do have their own website, as well), and bought the pairs that I was looking for, after reading reviews from customers who ended up becoming Lildy fans in the same manner. These jeggings are SO comfy that you can literally wear them 24/7, and even for activities that require a broad range of movement (splits, high kicks). I wear them to sleep, because I’m at that stage in my life where I’m too lazy to change in & out of clothes constantly (just turned 47). I have a “uniform” that works for me (wifebeater & jeggings), and it’s nice to be able to simply add accessories, outerwear & shoes, and voila….I’m dressed to go outside. 👌 Prior to finding the Lildy jeggings @ CVS, I was wearing leggings, but would wear a mini over them solely for the coverage to hide the cameltoe. Now, I don’t have to do that because the material for the jeggings is thicker. I save myself from needing to put on an extra garment that constantly shifts from movement, and requires additional labor to adjust & launder. Dressing is much easier, and I effortlessly look pulled together. I always wore bootcut or flared pants (they were more flattering for my silhouette), but these jeggings fit so well that I feel confident about how I look in them (can’t say the same for the ones from Walmart), and unlike w/ flared or bootcut bottoms, these tuck into knee-hi boots w/o any of that extra fabric, and make looking cute such a non-job. Highly recommended, if you feel that the cut will work for your body shape. Great value for the price—you’ll get hooked & end up coming back for more colors & patterns. (For reference, I’m just under 5’2”, ~110 lbs., 28” waist & 28” inseam.) UPDATE: (May 2022) I’m now on my SEVENTH pair of Lildy jeggings! 👖 Just got the olive green solid color. Made just like the rest. I LIVE in these—you can exercise & sleep in these jeggings, and it’s nice that they look like “real pants.” 😆 The grey ones look fairly refined, almost like twill pants, and you just pull’em up like they’re you’re favorite pair of leggings, but might be fine for a “casual nice” work dress code. 🥰

Because I do not usually spend this kind of money on clothing and am pretty unreliable with completing online returns, I read every review I could find on multiple websites before I selected my size. I still got it wrong (lol). I don’t usually leave reviews, but there is a LOT of conflicting information about the sizing of these jeans out there and I want to share what I found to be true.When selecting your size, I recommend taking two factors into consideration:1. What is your body shape/type – i.e. where do you carry your weight? If you are someone with more of an hourglass shape or more thick in the thighs/butt, I would follow the guidance of most of the reviews and size up. If you are someone like me with a small/medium butt and legs, I would recommend selecting either your normal size, or one size down.2. How do you want the jeans to fit – i.e. do you want a relaxed fit or a tighter fit? For reference, I am around 5’7, 122 pounds, and usually a size 26/size 2 in pants. The size 26 Wedgies fit me perfectly right out of the bag. However, based on other reviewers’ experience, 100% cotton jeans will relax/mold to your body over time and use. If I kept the size 26, they would have evolved to more of a “boyfriend” style relaxed fit on me. I personally wanted a snugger fit. I then ordered the size 25. They were tight on me and not the most comfortable right out of the bag (they also made my butt look smaller which was the opposite of what I was hoping). However, after wearing them for a few hours, especially sitting in them, they relaxed and fit me exactly the way I envisioned.I attached pictures of me in both and indicated the size I am wearing in each photo. This is what each pair looked like immediately after opening the package - I did not take a photo of the size 25 after they relaxed, but trust me when I say, they're worth it! Hope this helps someone!

They are high waisted which I like, and they offer quite a bit of control even though they are extremely comfy. I’m 5’ 5 and weigh 205lbs and the XL fit perfectly. The length is exactly what I wanted - approx 6” above the ankle. I love them so much I ordered another pair within 30 minutes of putting them on!

Being a mom buying a perfect pair of jeans is very challenging. I ordered several and these fit me right in all places. I am 5 foot seven and 140 pounds for reference the material is soft and yet they are quite snug but not too tight. They cover, in all the right places I’ll like that I don’t need a belt. They did not slide down. The zipper is solid metal.

Being a woman withvery weird measurements (smaller waist than hips, hip dips, but big thighs, no butt, etc.) I have never EVER been able to find leather pants that didn’t absolutely cut off my stomach’s circulation (even XL-XXL), they always seem to fit my legs and then nothing else. I’m usually a size 12-14 in jeans/pants, for reference. I am also 5’6, 200ish pounds so I’m not a small girl. These though!? The perfect stretch for those who bloat throughout the night after a few drinks or dinner and want the extra room without suffocating. I feel i could have maybe just gotten the L instead of the XL but I was scared lol. Anyways, they have no smell, they DO make a lot of noise but who cares!? They’re so cute and comfy. I wish they had these in brown too :( GET EM!!!!

Being in my late 50's, it's tough to find a decent pair of shorts that are too long, too short, too high waisted...it's always something not quite right. Heading to the West Coast to hike Death Valley and the Grand Canyon (finally!) so need some shorts that look decent, are comfortable, and made of a tough material. Definitely found it with these shorts! Perfect length, actually fits comfortably, the material is also a bit stretchy, and the material is just thick enough to be tough without being too hot.I'm 5'9" and usually wear a size 4 or 6 depending on the cut. I went with a 7 on these since we'll be hiking & climbing. Out of the 6 pairs of shorts I ordered, the two from Union Bay are the only keepers!

Being taller than the average woman, my wife was keen to order these “long” pants with hope for a reasonable fit. At the time of ordering, there were no length measurements although there is a size chart shown now. She went with medium based on what she would normally order based on her 6’ height and 34” inseam but the leg length is really not designed for those with longer legs. The pants are still very comfy to wear and the waist band is thick and well made. She’ll mostly be wearing these for lounging around the house but with consideration to the leg length, she gives these 4 stars.

Better than expected. The fabric is soft and thick in a good way. Small fits perfect on 5’7 height and 120lb. Would recommend for walking/hiking outside.

Better than shorts for a classier look. Only thing that helped me survive 95 degree temps in Europe this summer. So loose and breathable, I bought 9 colors! I was so comfortable and it kept the hot sun off my legs. I'm 5'1", 116 lbs. I hemmed them a bit and after washing them in cold and line drying them, they still want to shrink. Stretching them back into shape while wet seems to do the trick. In fact the only reason for four stars instead of five is that the care instructions are not clear that it should be dry clean only. So my recommendation is as if you need to hem them, wash them first and then see how much you need to take off after. If you're tall and they fit you perfectly out of the package then I would definitely just dry clean them.

Bitttccch (you're not a bitch, I'm just using it as a expression) if you haven't bought these pants then you need to. Now I've always had a problem with finding my size-- I always bought too big or too small. I am 5"5, 194 lbs w/ the dimensions of 33-42-25 (waist-hip-thigh). I ordered the size 14 and it fits me snugly, however not tightly bc its got enough stretch for you to pop a very wide street squat, do a high kick, etc. They are long so you can roll em up but that's ok. They are soft material and they don't feel restricting. You should know your measurements before buying if you are not sure what size in jeans you are, you can even ask if you aren't sure what size to get. But if not, try going with your normal size

Black and white so rich. This reasonably priced pant is super light weight, flows and comfortable. The pattern was well printed on the fabric. I felt very stylish. I'm 5'4" and wore a 1" espadrille and it was not dragging. I will get a lot of wear out of these pants. Purchased a L as recommended by Amazon. I'm usually a 10-12 jean. I felt like maybe I could be a M but the generous size made more comfortable and flows. So all good.

Black extra small xs fits me 5'2" 104lbs currently, with 25in waist(for high rise pants only). Inseam is roughly 28.5in which is why I must wear 4 inch heels with these pants. I do have long torso and short legs though. They are definitely more bell bottoms than flare pants. I never had a pair of bell bottoms before so when I first tried these pants on I wasn't sold on not being able to see my feet at all. I had to Google and found out it's normal to not see much of your shoes if you're wearing bell bottoms or wide legged pants lol As I tried more tops with these pants, I started to like them. These are truly high-waisted and have a very flattering cut. The fabric is relatively thinner but drapes very well. I don't think it would wrinkle easily. Think spring and summer pants. I tried out different tops with the pants since I never had bell bottoms before, and I found that bodysuits and crop tops probably work the best.Note there are only three belt loops in total.I ended up liking these pants a lot. Sexy and slimming and gives the illusion of longer legs. 4 stars instead of 5 because left pant leg is about 1/5 inch (5mm) longer than right pant leg and ever so slightly bigger leg opening.

Body Type: 5'6 female 125lbs (curvier bottom) Size ordered: Small These pants a little big in the waist but since they're baggy cargo pants, I think they still work - I'm hoping that they'll shrink a bit in the wash. They are super comfy and may be my new go to pant but I'm pretty disappointed that the color is a rich army green vs a muted, faded green as shown in some of the photos. Some reviews mention the bottom of the pants being tight but mine are super loose which is want I wanted! Overall, I think these are a great price conscious option for an in trend pant (I may even buy all 3 other colors!)

Currently 21 weeks pregnant, 5' 9", 145#. this is my second baby so my belly is a lot bigger this time around and I needed maternity scrubs. I ordered these in medium (my pre-pregnancy Wonderwink size) and the Cherokee maternity scrubs in a small. I found that the Cherokee maternity scrubs were very well fitted except for the fact that the belly band was way too tight and by the time I was nine months they would be impossible to wear. I have the same problem with these scrubs as other reviewers that the space in between the tummy and your legs is very big, however, once I put them on with a maternity shirt you can't tell the difference anyway. The good thing about them being a little big is I definitely have room to grow and they are super comfortable. Far more comfortable than the Cherokee were. I was going to try them in a small but I ultimately decided that the medium would fit me the best all the way through my pregnancy.

Debo decir que este pantalón ha superado mis expectativas (tanto que ya pedí otro color). Due to my shape, I struggle a great deal to find pants that fit. Usually my only options are granny styles or looks that are too risque for work. I checked the size chart and ordered a 12 to fit my hips, knowing it would be big in the waist. Of course, the stats help when reading reviews, so: 29.5" waist and 45" hips, 5'5" tall and 160 lbs. You can see in the first photo how loose the waist was. A quick adjustment with my sewing machine fixed the fit, and overall I'm pleased with how these pants look. Eventually I may have a go at letting down the hem to get a little more length out of them. For us ladies with significant curves, these pants require very minimal tailoring to achieve a lovely fit. At this price point, I'm thrilled to get such a professional look. One star off because they're extremely plastic feeling inside (though not outside). Overall, I'd highly recommend for curvy girls with basic sewing skills.

Even when I saw that these Cropped Corduroy Pants were 100% polyester I was not dissuaded from ordering them. This is because they are from Yesno, a brand that I own pieces from and wear often. If I didn't know that these were polyester pants, I wouldn't know. The fabric feels nice and soft, if pressed I would have guessed that they are a cotton blend. As is usual with this brand, these pants are very well made. They have roomy slash pockets in front and an interesting dropped yolk detail at the back waistband. The belt loops are nice and large so that a wide belt can be worn with them. The waist is mostly elastic and very stretchy, and the zipper and buttons at the front are nice quality. I'm very happy with the way these pants are constructed, the feel of the fabric and the rustic brown color. Sadly, for all I love about these pants they do not fit properly. They fit me, just not properly. There is a dropped crotch which was not mentioned in the listing. So if I have the pants fastened at my belly button or just above, the crotch is hanging two or three inches below that. This makes the rear end look weird because it puckers horizontally by the back seam. They do look better if I pull them up, but then the waist is right under my bra line. Even though a dropped crotch is not my preference, I would still wear them except the fit is not comfortable either. The back seam just rests against my hiney and somehow feels tight... Which is very strange since I'm pretty flat back there. So, even though the fit is not working for me these pants will probably work for others and I think are not a bad deal at $34.99. If you have a longer torso and more booty, these might be your pants! For reference, I am 5'8" 190 lb, and ordered the XL.
