Glad I took a chance on these, they are superb value.11-14-24 Bought again, still like, added photos. Even though the length seems short via measurement (I normally want at least a 30 inch inseam for my height) the length stretches during wear and is longer than the flat measurements. This pair I measured the inseam at 27" - fabric stretches so easily I think I stretched it a little to get the 28" measured before.

Favorite Jeans & affordable! First time ordering jeans online & after I’ve lost 40+ lbs, at that! Loves: I am 5’8” and these go below my ankle, so I can cuff them at my ankle, if I wanted & wear cute bootie shoes. I have SKINNY legs and they are very fitting & flattering! Material, zipper, & button all feel very durable. All 4 pockets are REAL! Also, the price is not bad at all compared to other pairs of jeans on here with much thinner material. Possible inconveniences: Not a lot of stretch in the waist area, but it works. I’m 168-170 lbs and a size 31 was suggested . I got a size 30 because all other stretch jeans I own, usually loosen up throughout the day; idk if these will, honestly. The material is on the thicker side. If my thighs and calves weren’t skinny, these would not fit, so they are SKINNY. I could have felt very comfortable in a 31, but I am expecting to lose additional weight, so these will do, but whatever size is suggested to you, these are true to size in the waist. BUY THEM!

Feel great.. Fit is great. Better than I hoped.. Very well Pleased.. I'm 5'6" wear size 10.. I have size 12 that I was wearing but others said go a size down.. so I bought a size 10.. they fit wonderfully a size 12 would have been way to large.. Dont like to order with out trying on.. These were GREAT! Everyone said get one size smaller-- I did and they were correct....

Feel just like Lululemon leggings without the cost. Love the pockets and they r not see through like most thin leggings are. My new favorite pants. I’m 5’7 270 and got a 3xl because of the reviews and I think a 2xl would be ok too.

Feel like butter on skin. Soft n smooth. I love them. I wear a 14/16 in US pants n ordered the queen. Fits nice n comes up over navel n stays put. Material is not very thick but also not the thinnest. You can wear summer or winter. I love the length as well I'm 5'6 ish and it's a perfect length.

Feel very well made and soft. They have a nice weight to them so they look and feel luxurious. Very breathable. A little short (I’m 5’7”) but easy to wear low or high waist. Want them in more colors now!
Feels cheaply made and the button fell off before I even put them on. They are too long but I’m 5’0 so it was expected.

FINALLY ! I am 5'2" 112 lbs. Finding pants that fit is like searching for the holy grail. These democracy jeans in optic white size 4 looked like they were custom made for me. Perfect at the waist and slim not skinny at the leg. Rolled up=a nice crop look...rolled down=perfect length. (when I ordered in blue the fit was too tight...skinny jean...go figure)

Finally a pair of flair jeans for tall girls! I got a 4Long. And they fit like a dream. I am 5'11" and 145lbs. I really wanted long flairs to wear with boots, but kept coming up short (wink). These fit perfect

Finally found a great jean jacket! I've tried so many and didn't like the length, shoulders, stretch, huggable arms, color, pockets, thin/thick ratio...this one checks all the boxes! I'm 5'3, 150 lbs, 36DD & usually prefer Lrg size for fit reference. :)

Finally found the perfect jeans that covers my mom pouch and fit comfortably. I’m a size 11/13 and got the 12. Im 5”7

Finally high waisted jeans that are actually high waisted. They fit wonderfully, above my belly button and comfy all around. They're tight and stretchy. These will make your butt look good even if you don't have one (me) :) Would recommend :)

Finally the vintage style pants I've been searching for! It's hard to find pants that aren't high waters on me these days. Everything always seem to stop above my ankle. I love high waist but wanted a true mom fit style . I have a pair body shape and these compliment my shape so nice. I'm 5,7 and 140lbs and ordered a 6 . I should've sized up . These pants fit but I could stand a tad bit more space around my waist. I will order again I hope these pants dont go outta style.

Finally, I found a pair of denim shorts that I can comfortable wear year round! Generally, I avoid denims because they are difficult to fit properly and are too hot to wear in the warm climates I always live in. But, I always thought denims look good and go with just about every top. This pair of Awuka denim shorts solved my problem. The material is very stretchy and doesn't bind anywhere. The cut is modest being high waisted and with the hem length. The pockets are deep enough. The stitching is good. I did trim off the fray strings at the hem as that's not me. Importantly, the material is not hot. I machine washed and tumbled dry and it didn't shrink. The small fits me quite well. My waist is 28 inches, weight is 120 lbs and height is 5'5". I would purchase this again.

Finally, some knee length shorts! That is what I really like about them. So much so that I also got them in black and navy. These shorts remind me of the ones we wore back in the day, and I was happy to have found them. There are a lot of different shorts on this listing, so it can be confusing. This review is for the white shorts with the buffalo check print that you can fold up. From my experience, this style is the longest they carry. I found them to run a little loose, and there is elastic in the back. They sent a little belt with it, but I haven’t even taken it out if its package yet. I recommend following their size chart. These white shorts are not see through with nude underwear! I am including a photo of me wearing them today and I tucked the back of my HOT PINK shirt into them so you can see for yourselves. The one thing I personally dislike about these shorts is the brown buffalo check material that is sewn on. Thankfully I can wear it unfolded and not have that show. Unfortunately, it kinda does show through just a tiny bit, although my husband said he didn’t notice. Also it buttons where you fold it up, and the buttonhole is not done that well. I am letting these things slide since I am happy to have a decent pair of white shorts that are long enough. For reference, I am 5’9”, 134 lbs, and I bought size small.

Finally...a pair of jeans that fit perfectly and feel amazing! The Silver Jeans Co. Women's Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Trouser Leg Jeans are a high-end high-quality pant. They are well made with "real" denim that is heavy weight and durable. The double button and zipper fly is attractive. The fit is amazing. I don't have many curves at 5'6" and 120 lbs. Most women's size pants fit terribly so I am doomed to wearing leggings 90% of the time or juniors clothing. These jeans are the answer to my pant needs! There is a very slight stretch to the denim...ever so slight...that is a really nice plus. I will likely buy an additional pair in another color. These are fabulous! Bravo!! I am so happy to have found a pair of jeans that fit great, look fantastic, are high quality, durable, and well made.

First dress was too small in bust. Large was ordered which is my usual size. I decided to try a 3-x which was way too big all over. Exchanging for 2-x. First I will say the color does not look like the picture. (I ordered the Jenna) but not a deal breaker for me. love how they fit and that they are long. Personally I would love about 1/2 inch to an inch longer but these will be just fine. For reference I’m 5’10 and wear a 18.

First of all these are just jeggings. They were $26.00 and the seller charged $23.00 to shipped them. Although I have Amazon prime some how a shipping fee was charged. Not cool! On top of it all they are 3 sizes too small. My 13yr old daughter couldn't even fit them.

First of all these are super cute. I am 5’2 so I have to buy short length pants otherwise they are just so long. I got these today and tried them on and they are SOOOO LONG! first of all, ignore my terrible pics…but those give you an idea of my body shape. i usually stick to one brand of jeans bc i’m 37, hate my body, and don’t understand how to dress myself lol. anyway, i keep seeing these ads so i just figured i’d at least try them. i will say, they’re very comfortable. i’m 5’3, about 138lbs but have big hips/butt. i genuinely don’t like wearing tight clothing and need to feel fabrics before buying but these are good. they’re high waisted, which i must have missed in the description bc im dumb, but that’s fine. since i am on the shorter side, they are a bit long, but nothing i can’t manage by rolling the bottom. since they are longer, they seem a bit bigger in the calf area (which you can see in pics). it’s nothing that would make me want to return since i know these will shrink and fit me just fine.

First of all, the important part is I am 5’10” & 120lbs (in those photos), I bought a medium, tall, the 33” inseam, in white, khaki and black. And I am only wearing flats. Posting several pics to see the fit, plus for proof its not possible to take a bad pic in Europe. Anyway, I bought these to wear on our three week trip. I wanted something I could beat up but still look dressy & fabulous. Since we had never been to Europe and only brought a backpack each, I was really nervous about my clothes. But let me tell you, I would buy these pants all over again. I honestly felt like I one upped all the younger, high fashion girls in Milan and Capri. Also, my husband had me vacuum seal my clothes in my backpack. So they were (neatly) crammed into a super tight bag for three weeks. They went through six flights, unknown bus and trains and multiple ABNBs. Every time I pulled them out, they were wrinkle free. They are so soft, I actually slept in them, several times. They STILL do not have stretched out knees. They cleaned up super easy and air dried every where we went (cuz they don’t have dryers in Europe). I wore the WHITE in the RAIN on the streets of ROME (i feel really spoiled saying that) and figured that would be the ultimate test. I honestly figured I would be throwing them away when we got back. After 19,000 steps in the rain, the bottoms where they hit my tennis shoe heel had some discoloration after I washed them. But there has been no shrinkage and I still wear them. I wanted a loose, classy, palazzo look and the medium, tall did exactly that. I bought the small, tall and it was too tight on my bottom for what I wanted, so I gave them to my daughter who is about five pounds smaller. But the smalls did fit. The medium doesn’t feel like they are going to fall off, I felt confident in them everywhere we went. And the hips don’t look like they aren’t cut for a girl my size. I’ve purchased much more expensive pants and didn’t get 1/2 of what I am getting out of these. My only wish is they made them with a sewn in front seam and a non-yoga unwrapped waste for a more professional look. If they did, I’d probably buy the company.

First off I want to say these are not short friendly. These are definitely for my 5 foot three and up girls because they are very long as you can see in the picture but I do love the adjustable straps if you learn how to button them just right as you can see, you can create more of a cuff look which fits a little better but these are perfect for a taller girl or even a petite woman and a 3 inch to 5 inch heel. The waistband is absolutely perfect. It fits perfectly on my smaller size waist while keeping the jeans very baggy and form. Perfect for any streetwear or grungy outfit.

First off I will these fit me perfectly. I got size medium and I’m 5’4”, 130 lbs. they are meant to be high waisted, it sits comfortably right above my belly button and the length is perfect. If you’re average height or taller these probably won’t fit right. I’m very impressed with the quality and weight of the fabric. Sage green is true to the pictures and no issues with them being see through. Definitely ordering another pair

First off I would say that are the most comfortable stretch dress pants I have come across. They have cute little pockets in the front and elastic waistband. This product is true to size. I am 5'3. I weigh 165 lb and I have big hips and got a large but I could have got a medium.

First off I’m on the shorter side (5 foot 3) and weigh 120 pounds. I love the flare look but usually I have to have them hemmed which is annoying, these are perfect! They barely touch the ground with my tennis shoes on! So cute and the material is so soft and there is slight tummy control as well. The back pockets are so adorable and the scrunch on the butt is perfect (some pairs are too much and ride up) these don’t ride! I ordered another pair! So cute and really fun colors as well.

First off the sizing on this was great as I am a 29” waist and got the medium and as you can see I have this up on my natural waist and I’m on one of the middle holes so hopefully I’ll be able to use it lower on my hips without issues too. I love the buckle because it’s truly an accessory that elevates your outfit rather than just some thing functional. The faux leather is smooth and clean and doesn’t take away from the buckle’s design.

First off, I LOVE these jeans! Secondly, the reviews I read on here were a huge help in sizing. It’s weird because this are definitely Women’s jeans, but the sizing is in Juniors. For reference I am 6’0, 170-175 lbs, and a solid Women’s size 10/30, which would translate to a Juniors size 11. Per the suggestion of previous reviews, I reluctantly sized down to a 9. The last thing I wanted was for the jeans to fit for an hour, only to stretch beyond repair. I ordered a size 9 with 34” inseam. I am SO happy I did! When I pulled it out the bag I thought to myself, there’s NO WAY this is gonna work. But I squeezed myself into the initially doubtful fit. Wisely I decided to wear them around the house for a couple hours, w/o removing tags (just in case.. again, because of reviews). And to my pleasant surprise this wonderful pair of jeans decided to form to my figure in the best ways possible. So if you’re in doubt and you got some junk in the trunk, size down and trust the process. Because I could totally see if I had bought these in an 11, I wouldn’t like them now. Also the 34” inseam does almost hit the floor for me, but the next pair I order will be a 36”, just so I have some extra length.

First off, the fabric is different from what I expected. It's a denim, canvas-type material. However, they are so comfortable and cute! These pants exceeded my expectations. They are perfectly baggy and look great with a body suit or cropped tank. These cargos are great pair of pants at a reasonable price for a trendy style. I'm 5'3, 115#, and wearing an XS.

First off, the waist is tight on these — I’m usually a size 6 and I ordered the small and right now they’re too tight to comfortably wear. Also, I am 5’1” and the jogger bottom ends up making these look a bit clowny on me with an inflated bottom. All that is a shame though, because otherwise I really like the pants. They’re cute and look dressy while still comfortable.

First off, these are a solid black jean (not the worn look, as shown in the advertising pics). I love the rich black color and the fact that the fleece lining is also solid black. The lining is so soft, it is like wearing your favorite blanket. These are pretty true to size, although the waistband is a little tighter than I like and they are pretty long (I'm only 5'2"). That said, they are very comfy and fit throughout my curves nicely. These will be great for winter.

First off. 14 day delivery. Do not order last minute! Secondly I was worried about the quality but it is very nice. No weird seams. No snags. Photos of belly are to reference the fit. It goes well over my belly button but I knew it might as I am very short waisted. 5’5” 216 38G it fits really good. It is my new airplane travel garment!

First pair of "real" jeans after over a 100lb weightloss. I bought the 12L, as I am tall and have been fitting into a 12/14 legging and jegging. They fit perfectly! If you are over 5'7" I would definitely suggest the longer length. I am a hair shy of 5'8" and did not want it to stop oddly above my ankle. They are very comfortable and I was able to squat to the ground in them, easily. The color is also nice and flattering.

First pic I was bout to take it off that's why it's unbuttoned but it fits I gotta xl I'm 5'4 204 big breast it fits and it's stretchy definitely get it I'm between a large and xl depending on the outfit

First problem: the description says "mid-rise", yet the picture is of jeans that are CLEARLY LOW RISE. Honestly I was looking for low rise, but decided I would settle for mid-rise if that's what I received. Ordered a size 7 first (5'5", 135 lbs) which was WAY too small, plus the tags on what they sent me all said "High-rise". Returned those and ordered size 9 thinking the high-rise part was just a fluke. Nope. They sent me high-rise again (as can be seen in the picture) and on top of that, they are slightly loose in the waist, which means they would be falling down in no time. Sending these back for a refund. Won't be ordering again.

First red flag. The pants arrived in the clear warehouse plastic bag. No Amazon packaging or added protection. Tried them on- they’re at least a full size too big. I’m a size 6, 7, or 28. 150lbs hourglass shape. Tiny waist big hips. The waist actually fit alright. The pockets, zipper area and thighs were baggy. Thought maybe I could wash them to tighten up. But then I saw a HUGE HOLE on the inner thigh… if I do replace I would get a size 5. And make sure it comes in Amazon packaging. Genuinely disappointed

First thing you need to know is that every pattern and color has a different fabric makeup which changes the look and feel of them. This is why the reviews have huge outliers. I bought the grey polka dot to start with, and they are polyester and spandex. I got the XXL based on reviews and they are huge but cozy. They did have a bit of a cheap look (shiny fabric and exposed stitching). I decided to order the solid black in just XL and they are 15% cotton and 85% polyester! Different feeling and they are also shorter but look better quality. I am 5’ 6” and 195lbs. Just had a baby and carry my weight in my hips. The XL is fine and these things stretch. I am concerned about the exposed stitches in the grey pair. They came this way and look like they are about to rip. I’ve worn them for a week and washed them twice. They are super comfortable and I just hope they last. I am rating the black pants 5 stars.

First things first, I have a Brazilian butte given by God and thighs that can leg press 400lbs but still look not so great. So I look for something that has me comfortable, fashionable and flattering. These get a 100% for comfort, as cozy as Jammies. They are a soft thin material. Not see through but weightless and offer flexibility. I will give an 85% for flattery because the butte looks ok n from the side but the stomach does not have any tucking. No muffin top and great with casual and dressy. I would not wear a crop top but a shirt that covers my lower stomach. All in all I give it a YES 🙌🏼, worth cost and trendy/comfortable enough to make a super cute outfit.

First time ever that I have ordered a pair of jeans online. I have always been one to go and try them on in the store before buying, but with the stretch waistband and spandex in them, I decided to take a chance. I am 5'4" and 180-ish so I ordered the X-Large. I have a problem with so many in the pants line being too long due to my height. Figured with these it might work out to be just about right since they are supposed to be cropped. I was right! They are nearly a normal length on me, and I do not mind that it alters what the style was meant to be at all, lol. I dress for comfort, not fashion. These jeans are very soft and have a forgiving stretch that makes them super comfy to wear. I was surprised at how thick and heavy in weight they are. They will be nice and toasty warm when cooler weather gets here and in the winter. First pair of jeans too that I have ever been able to tuck in a shirt and did not have a spare tire hangover at the top of the jeans. These jeans are well made and look like they will last a long time. I can highly recommend these jeans. If I see a good sale on them, I will be grabbing a second pair, I like them that much! Very happy I took the chance on them.

First time I even got an Amazon brand jean (normally I’m an express girl). I’m 5’7” and 120 so I purchased size 0 regular. The long would have been a mistake! Make sure to measure your proper waist bc these are medium/high rise. I’m gonna wear these with ankle booties, heels, my Tom’s or cowgirl boot. Thinking of getting more colors.

First time wearing boot cut jeans in years and these did not disappoint!! I would like to say that you should size down 1 or 2 sizes, I’m 5’4 and weigh 123lbs and went for the small, I had to use a belt as their was a lot of waist room left, I believe the xs would’ve probably been better for me. Other than that I loooooovvve these jeans, they are so cute, I wore them for Valentine’s Day and got tons of compliments on them!!

First, and most importantly, they fit me really well around the waist. These are the first pants I've owned since having a baby that I can say that about, but these are able to somehow hold onto my belly and not fall down or dig in. I think maybe it's the stretchy material and wide the ruched waistband. I'm 5'10" so finding pants long enough for me is a challenge. These are good because they are cropped and I don't think there's a particular length they have to reach like regular pants. That said, these are longer than I expected. They have not made me feel like I'm too tall to wear them. Second, and almost as important as fitting well, they are comfortable. Like mega comfortable, because they are super soft and lightweight and stretchy. If you are looking for structured pants, look elsewhere. If you want pants that you can get down in the floor to play with your kids, wear all day painlessly, and not bother to change into pajamas at bedtime because they are just that comfortable, these are for you. Third, how they look. I personally don't know what to do with these style wise. I don't own any short or cropped shirts (I'm tall haha). I think they would look cute if the waistband was more visible, but I can't style it that way without updating my wardrobe. So for the looks of the pants, I've knocked off a star simply because I don't know what to wear with them that would be a cute outfit. Fourth, the functionality of these pants surprised me. They went through the wash well with the settings I use for all my clothes (cold/cold wash, extra low dry for 1 hour, spin without heat for a while to cool). The fit didn't seem to change after washing (I have only washed once so far). The pockets are generous in size, my (fairly large) phone fits all the way inside with plenty of extra room at the top to keep it from falling out when I sit. I can even do things like sit on the floor and not only does the phone stay securely in my pocket the whole time, even when getting up, it is comfortable and I don't feel the phone jabbing my hip or anything when I move or sit. Also, the pants stay out on my waist even when adding the weight of the phone to the pocket. Over time, the waistband may get slightly crooked from the uneven load, but it doesn't make the pants fall down or hang cattywompus. I haven't found any lounge pants or even jeans that perform so well at comfortably and securely holding my phone in a pocket. Usually they do well at one (if that!) but not both. I am a SAHM and these pants work really well for my lifestyle. They are very casual, so I don't know if I feel comfortable styling them for wearing on outings. I've primarily used them at home. If you like this wide leg flowy style, for sure give them a try.

First, disappointing that this pair was still long even though I ordered a “Short.” For reference I am 5’2. Second and most importantly, returning this because as I was trying it on I suddenly got stabbed by something sharp— come to find out it was a broken needle that was left in the seam of the pants.

First, I bought the denim pair then loved them so much that I bought the white pair! I did size up since I like mine to fit loose. Great when washed and air dried - didn’t shrink! I’m 5’6”, 155lbs, with big hips and ordered an XL.

First, I cant believe I am sharing such candid pics but I feel us thicker ladies never can tell what the right size is to buy so I hope this helps y'all. (This is my dads acct btw) Ok, to I am 5'6" and currently weight 182 lbs, all of my weight is in my tummy and thighs, I am only a 38B. I go between a 12 and 14 in jeans. I'm pretty sure I remembered some of the reviews saying to size up. I originally ordered size 16 bc I didnt want them skin tight, but they were WAY to big. Sent those back and tried a 12. PERFECT. I can still easily get in and out of the front pockets and I don't feel squished by the waistband when I sit down. I am absolutely getting more of these in different colors so long as they don't shrink in the washer and dryer. If that happens I will for sure update. Good luck ladies!

First, I only gave it 4 stars because the pants came with no hole for me to button them close. Overall, as you can see in the pictures, I love them and will not return them. First, I was really surprised at how soft these are. I tried them on and then kept them on (I usually wash everything before I wear). They also didn’t have the weird chemical smell that a lot of clothing has. I really love the fit, and the long pockets are flattering. The pants have a baggy barrel cut which is generally not flattering on me. However, you can see in my third photo that you only notice the barrel cut when I pull the denim out to the side on one leg, but the other leg lays flat. They also fit true to size. I am 5’6, 140 pounds and size 6 fits perfectly. I got these with a 20% off coupon and a flash sale, so the value for the price is fantastic.

First, let me state that I have a tiny mirror so my pictures aren’t great. I am also 65, and you can judge for yourself whether I’m too old for this trend. My philosophy is you are almost never too old, but you do you. I purchased the Frida in a 26, which is my normal size and they fit perfectly. I’m only 5’3” and 100 lbs, but have longer legs, so while these are a bit long I like the length. They don’t drag on the ground with casual shoes but can still be worn with heels or boots. I like the front seam detail and the pockets are roomy for women’s denim. I’m loving this baggier style, although I think since I’m small I’ll wear something tighter on top rather than a blousy top and baggy bottom. Live and learn no matter your age. They are super comfortable. I’m usually ready to put sweats on when I get home-COVID ruined me for real clothes, but I can wear these relaxing. I am, in fact, resting on the couch wearing these as I type. Another good buy from Amazon the Drop. I would say this is my new favorite place to get denim.

For comparison: I am 5’5” and I am usually 120 lbs but post holiday I’m rocking 125-130 lbs. I normally wear US size 4 or 27 in pants and size medium bikini bottoms. I have wide hips, some junk in the trunk, and shorter legs. Product: With the extra winter weight hanging on tight, mostly due to my love of chocolate and avoidance of the outdoors when the temperature is under 60, I decided to order medium Grapent jeans. I did not want the pants to be too tight and hinder my couch potato winter lifestyle. That was a mistake. The provided size chart recommended a size small but I have trust issues when it comes to size charts so I went up a size. Not only are the jeans plenty big in the waist, maybe for 8/10/12, but there is quite a bit of stretch in the material. Also, the fabric lost a bit of shape after a day of running errands. I fully regret not getting the recommended small because the material is very soft and stretchy so I think I would love them if they fit better. That’s my bad! The fabric is not a true denim but rather a medium weight cotton/poly blend. There are functional hip pockets as well as back pockets. I especially like the thigh rip because it is a different style than my other ripped jeans. As for length, I had to roll the cuffs to wear with sneakers. I’m normally a 29” inseam and these jeans were probably 30-31”. These waist just qualifies as “high” landing just past my belly button. The rear area was the only fitted part with the legs being looser. Recommendation: These mom jeans are stylish and super comfortable. I recommend going by the size chart in this case and rest easy knowing the fabric has plenty of stretch. The only negative thing I found was the tag instructs to hand wash while the product page advertises machine washable. I am going to machine wash but hang to dry and hope for the best. I really like these jeans and would recommend to a friend!

For context I'm 5'7 190# 52 yo... I am very picky about what I wear for many reasons, first being I am a gen xer in my 50s which means I need to still be stylish and somewhat boho but respectable for my age! Iykyk! So I need my pants to be comfy, lightweight, extra cute but not too young and wearable to the office as much as home. I have tried at least a dozen different pairs of pants from Amazon this year and most of them have gone right back to the return pile. So when the shipment of these rust bread linen pants came I rolled my eyes and through the bag on my bathroom dressing table without a second thought. Fast forward to this morning... At the moment I tried them on a ray of light entered the room and there rose a choir of angels singing.... Yes! These are PERFECT in every way! Color is interesting, stylish yet subtle!! Wide leg not shapeless! Perfect hit at ankle! Pockets but not obvious! Elastic waistband provides support, not bulge!!! I love them enough and I have struggled enough to find the right pair that I decided to actually post a photo of me wearing them which made me cringe but I want you to be happy as I am right now! Omw to order one(or two) in every color....

For Reference I am ( 36C 162 lbs 5’4” size med / large ) Love the color on these khaki hue sweater pants, but what is most impressive is the wide range of sizes and lengths that is available. The size inclusivity for individuals who require a shorter inseam is Fantastic ! The only negative is that it is limited to only 2 colors ( black & brown ) I would love to see a wider range of color options. The sizing chart states the petite inseam as 26.8”. I measured mine to be a 27” inseam. The fabric is comprised of 50% Viscose, 28% Polyester and 22% Nylon. The material is soft and has a opaque appearance which falls right in between a knit and a sweater. It is not itchy but does offer a good amount of stretch and sight warmth. The elasticized waistband is 2” thick and offers a working drawstring for an adjustable fit. I did not find these to be a high rise as they sit right on the waist on me. What I did find is that the crotch area is elongated, which looks unflattering, but can be slightly corrected by rolling up the waistband as an option, which then will effect the length of the pants. The description refers to these as straight leg design, however the pants I received are straight just up to below the knee, it then flares out to a wide leg hem. What gives these pants some interest is the vertical outseam which runs the length of the pants from the waistband to the bottom hem. The seam in my opinion elevates the appearance of these pants so they can be utilized not only for casual or lounge purposes but with a pair of heels can be taken into the work place. Machine washable according to the care instruction label - cold delicate cycle but I found that hand washing has helped prevent shrinking due to the fabric makeup

For reference, I am 5’6” 142 lbs with a rectangular torso and thick thighs, I ordered a medium, as suggested, and I don’t have any complaints about the way they fit. They are snug on my body type, because I don’t have much of a waist, but if you are on the skinny side or have an hourglass figure, i’m sure it would be different. There’s nothing overtly wrong with the shorts, but they simply aren’t what I ordered. I included a picture of the color and style for reference, next to what they look like on. I went back and double checked what I ordered, thinking maybe there were two grays and I clicked on the wrong one - but I ordered correctly and didn’t see another one that looks like what I got that it could have been mixed up with. The shorts in the photo look like they’re made of soft, swing-y material, almost like pajamas. The fabric of the shorts I received is stiff and rough. It’s also almost a blue, and not at all the neutral gray pictured. They don’t lie flat against your body, but puff out. I’ve tried on worse shorts, and can always use these for yard work, but I don’t think I’d have looked twice at the real ones vs what I liked about the ones advertised. They aren’t sheer, they have good back coverage. I don’t understand the super heavy-duty waistband paired with an unnecessarily long and superfluous drawstring (it literally does nothing), but I guess it’s not hurting anything. Not worth the hassle of sending them back at this price point, but beware you’re not getting what you think you are.

For reference, I carry all my weight in my midsection so it's very difficult to find jeans that fit comfortably and don't show my apron belly. I typically wear a size 12 or 14 and am 5'1" tall, so length is also always an issue. I first ordered the $98 Free People barrel jeans in all the colors, hoping they would look cute. I tried one on and didn't even bother trying on the rest, I immediately returned them. Unless you're a size 2, the FP jeans were clownishly huge in the legs, but the denim was buttery soft. I am not typically satisfied with the quality of clothing on Amazon, but decided to try several barrel jeans because I wasn't having any luck from name brands. To my surprise, these fit GREAT! The fabric is a denim blend so not the same quality of designer fabric, but they are beyond flattering - comfortable stretch in the waist, slim through the hips, then a reasonable barrel flare with a tapered bottom. They skim the tops of my shoes so I have to cuff them up a little and they're perfect. Might hem them, but I hate losing the original stitching. I ordered 3 colors and love all of them. The Black is perfectly faded, the Deep Blue is a really nice medium blue, and I'm so happy they make the Retro Blue, which is just like the FP jeans with the slightest green tinge. I initially ordered a pair of barrel jeans from another seller, Grapent, and I kept them but the wash is not as nice, the pockets kind of stretch out, and these jeans are a better shape and quality of colors. If you're an apple shape and hard to fit, I really recommend these. I'm so happy to be able to buy into a trend for less than half the cost of the designer brands. What a find!!!

For reference, I’m 4’11. I weigh 205lbs. I purchased a size 11. There’s a lot of stretch but there’s a gap in the back. The jeans came up past my belly button. Loved that part but hate the gap. The jeans were pretty long, so the taller girlies shouldn’t have a problem.

For reference, I’m 5’1, 110 lbs. On my body, the jeans are mid rise, hitting just below my belly button. Would definitely recommend these jeans.

For starters, I’m 5’11, 125lbs, usually wear a 28 in jeans or a 4-8 depending on the brand. I was skeptical about these, I didn’t love the idea of pulling on jeans, however I have been pleasantly surprised. I ordered a size 6, with 32” inseam and they fit PERFECTLY. They are not soft like athleisure leggings, definitely a stretchy denim. They pull on easily, and provide a small amount of stomach support and flattening (but not uncomfortably tight). I have hips, a decent booty and large thighs & calves for my build. It makes finding skinny jeans difficult because they fit in the waist but are too tight on my thighs and claves, but these were comfortable and fit both thighs and calves well, without being too constricting. The other issue I run into as a fairly tall woman, is jeans length. The 32” inseam hit me EXACTLY where they need to, right at my ankle bone. Overall, I love these, and if they sold these in white, I would buy them immediately and they would likely replace my white Paige jeans.

Given that I am curvy (a pretty traditional pear shape with a smaller waist and larger hips, bottom, and thighs), I have always been wary of "skinny" jeans and have tended to stick with more boot-cut styles. After the rave reviews about these jeans for curvy women, I decided to try them. I am most often a size 8 in jeans, but I got these in a 10, as I thought that I would need the extra size due to the "skinny" cut.These jeans are a dark rinse with a slight whiskering. They are described as having a "flex and flatter fabric," and I would say that this is true--there is a nice stretch to them, and they comfortable hugged my curves. The length was perfect for me (5'4.5"), just past the ankles. I do think a size 10 was the correct size for me in this style, although as you can see from my photos, I did still have a slight gap to the waistband at the back. However, since these jeans have a hidden elasticity to the waist, the waistband did hug my body, and the small gap was barely noticeable. The waistband hit just above my hip bones and right at my belly button. These jeans were also flattering to the booty, with a nice placement to the pockets plus a curved yoke/shaping darts.I have machined washed/dried these jeans, and they seemed to turn out well with no shrinkage. Overall, these are definitely a nice pair of jeans for the curvy woman, and I would recommend me them, especially for those who have a bit less of a waist-to-hip ratio than I do.1/11/23 UPDATE:I still have these and they have become my favorite jeans! I've gained a little weight with menopause, so either that or the jeans shrinking slightly have helped them to fit better. They are comfortably stretchy and feel just right.

Good points: Very deep black (and they come with a warning that the colour might rub off on lighter textiles!) VERY stretchy if that's what you want Nice and long in the legs. I'm 5'11" and could wear boots or heels with these Modern boot cut (more on that later) Drawbacks: The FIT: I always wear a 12 in Lee jeans and these were a bit tight. I could wear them if I didn't mind the muffin top due to the fact they are smaller than any of my other jeans and the "midrise" is very low (to me, at least). If you are looking for mid-to-high, really, like me, do not buy these. The STRETCH: VERY stretchy. The kind of stretch that snaps in to hug your body no matter what. And, I don't know why, but that sort of pants always seems to get folds in the backs of the thighs (the front, too, if they are loose, but these are not). They are also very stifling in the heat. If, like me, you live somewhere where it is 30 degrees Celsius much of the year, do not order these. You're welcome! (Looked it up, that's 86 in Fahrenheit.) The CUT: As I said, these are modern boot cut. Maybe I am really looking for flares. 20 years ago, what we called boot cut flared out right below the knees and looked great. These grab me right down to mid-calf (so everyone can clearly see how knock-kneed I am - if my legs were straight, I'd be even taller!). Then they flare waaaaaay out. I find that pretty ugly on me. I guess they could work with boots if you have small calves (I do not). They are indeed huge through the ankles, but I think they would be pretty snug over boots in the lower calf. Also, they are on the low side of midrise, which is another huge negative for high-waisted me. Hope this helps!

Haven’t worn jeans in years, instead ankle-length, moderately supportive athletic leggings had become my mainstay. I am 5’2”, approx 115 lbs with tummy and let’s just say not massively firm from tush through to thighs — despite being a casual 3x/week runner... After trial and error, I have determined I am a 2S... Any bigget or longer and they would not hit my ankles in the perfect spot, and the waist and butt would be too loose... These things are A-MAZING... they provide support, smooth out my abundant lumpiness, keep my muffin top in check and feel great! I have yet to see how they stand up to linger wearing, whether they stretch out and wash well, but will back with an update once I do... While I certainly have obvious issues and a body befitting a 50-something, somewhat active woman battling the UNIVERSAL challenges of a changing physique (ahem) I am pleasantly surprised EVEN PLEASED with the magic of these jeans... I kinda feel like I am misrepresenting myself to the world (but in a good way, lol) .... Love these so much that I have been wanting to order them in GALA, but they are not available... So as a back up I ordered another in Cape Town— the wash highlight on my first pair was perfect and happily, the over all tone is darker than it appears in the product pic. But the second pair ordered was visibly larger in the thighs and lighter in color overall and so I am returning them and hoping to re-purchase one that fits like the first and will wait patiently and hopefully until they offer the GALA in 2S. If you’re on the fence I highly recommend you take a chance and order these… I would also recommend Levi’s gold label modern skinny Jean — very similar feel and fit and currently available in a very dark wash called immaculate… I have already purchased two of those and I’m very pleased… I never thought I’d have a jean wardrobe: I am thrilled! Thank you Amazon! Than you Levi’s 👍🏼🙏💕😎😍

Here's my wife for this review:I wanted a pair of jeans that I could be comfortable in and look a little up to date with the trends and I didn't want them to be blue necessarily. I saw these offered in a few colors and loved the olive green color. I also loved that these are high waisted and have a loose fitting to these. The straight legged barrel look is popular right now and honestly it's a comfortable style to wear. I ordered them in a size 10 which is my go to size. I'm 5'6 and 160 pounds. I think they could be a little baggier but they fit me in the waist nicely. They're a LOT of stretch to these jeans which adds to the comfort. I can roll the pant legs up to create a wide hem which is in style today or I can leave them long which fits me like a good hemmed pair of jeans would be. I do have shorter legs than most people so perhaps this would be above the ankle on most people which is also the style today. I also love that you could just cut the hem and leave it raw which is also the style of today!I haven't worn them for a long time so I don't know how the stretch factor is. If I see an issue I will back but for now I really am enjoying these jeans. They're assembled nicely. I do see a little hesitation in the zipper when I zip them up, but I believe it's because of the stretch in the jeans. The zipper lays fine and doesn't gap or anything. For the money, style, and comfort, get yourself a pair of these.

Herseas Sweatpants are a, "wear all the time, everywhere, comfortable sweatpants". I'm not usually a huge polyester fan, but these pants are beyond comfortable. Hey Amazon. This is an entirely unbiased and unpaid for product review. I got these for a cosplay I was doing (Pearl from Steven Universe). I originally meant to cut these into shorts however I kept them long because of weather. These came within a day with my student Prime which was excellent. The product was very well packaged and immediately I noticed how soft these were. They were the texture of a very thin velvet almost and the color stayed saturated even at my joints. Trying them on they fit all the way down to NY ankles. I'm 5'2" so I didnt really have any concerns, however, I believe a taller person would have problems with leg length because even for a short person these didn't go awfully long. Now although the material was very very soft it was also very thin. My butt was covered in my costume however I wouldn't recommend wearing these (at least the light colors) by themselves. Wearing standard underwear the pattern and crease of the underwear was very obvious and looked pretty bad. I think even with a thong it'd be way too obvious. So if you plan to wear these by themselves get a darker pair because the lighter pair won't satisfy your needs. With them being so thin they didn't keep in heat very well either and I was pretty cold even inside. Also, I developed a small hole near my crotch after dancing in them for a while, probably due to constant stretching. Overall I believe these are decent quality. I would consider buying a black pair as leggings if the material would be made a little thicker. If you need something for under a skirt or shorts then these are absolutely perfect. TDLR; Soft and well made but too thin

Hi! So okay, I have had a few pairs of these “soft, boho pattern flare pants” throughout my young life. I remember when they peaked back in early 2010s! So, if you see my photos, I already owned a few pairs in Red/Pink and Black/White. These pants are my favorite. They are so soft, such good quality. They feel like cotton, they’re pretty thick (and don’t show panty lines!), and they’re the perfect length on my 5’7 self, they are truly the best. I really wanted some in Blue, but I couldn’t find any in my area. So I decided to give Amazon a shot. I’m a bit disappointed. You may not be able to tell from my photos, but these pants are… different. The pattern is clearly laser printed. It doesn’t look as ‘real’ as the red and black ones. Also, the quality is much worse. It doesn’t feel like soft cotton, it feels more like polyester. The pattern does look good, but it’s just not the same. The fit is fine, but it’s more lightweight than my genuine boho pants. It’s also more see through!?! That’s not supposed to happen, IMO. Overall, they’re thinner, not as soft, and really cheaply made. But still, If you’re just going for a boho look, then go for it. I’d say, if you can, try to find the real deal boho flares in discount department stores.

So I'd keep that in mind if you don't have long legs. The only other thing I'd note about these is that they have NO belly control. I take comfort in having even a little (even though I have a flat stomach) but if you have a belly or are just self conscious, I'd be a little weary. I'd also note that if you don't have hips, these pants may be difficult to stay on you. I get the impression that these slide down on others because they slide a little on me. I absolutely love these. Look, I was one of those big baggy Hot Topic pants kids back in the 90s and early 2000s. I've tried for a long time to ensure the skinny jeans, slim fit jeans look. I'm done with it. I can't stand peeling off my pants or the discomfort of jeans. I bought these and I don't ever want to go back. I love these so much I bought a second pair immediately after the first arrived. I washed these and put them in the dryer on normal heat and they shrank a little, but enough that they still fit without hugging too close or cutting into me. I can still pull them away and have a good several inches. The weight on these is great. Airy for summer, but heavy enough for winter, and I've got enough room to throw on thermals underneath if I want to. They're perfect. So far the stitching has held up. For reference, I'm short and squat. I'm 5'4", 135 lbs. These don't drag when I walk which is a miracle. (I wear my pants at my belly button.) They're tapered at the very bottom so they're not too wide legged at the bottom. I was worried because wide leg pants on me looks terrible but these are cut in a way that's super flattering. I would say the only con? Is that the gray is a bit lighter than I expected based off photos but I love them anyway. I'll update if anything terrible happens but I intend to live in these. Absolutely wonderful pants lol.
I absolutely LOVE these. So soft more like silk my butt is huge so I got an XL I weigh 181 and I’m 5’6 the length is perfect. Will definitely be buying more 😍

I ADORED these pants when they first arrived. I’m 5ft 4.5in tall, 185 lbs, and ordered the XL. They were the perfect, breezy, floor-length pants of my dreams. They skimmed my problem area (my postpartum belly) gracefully and made my stomach look flatter. I washed them once on the cold delicate cycle and hung them to air dry and they shrank at least 2 or 3 inches. They used to touch the top of my feet, and now they are above my ankles. I’m not sure if I’ll wear them at all now - instead of lengthening my proportions, they make my legs look shorter and stubbier. Extremely disappointing and a waste of money. Save yourself the heartache! Unless you’re exactly 5’2, in which case they’d probably end up being perfect lol. Ladies, know your fabrics. Anything 100% rayon or viscose will shrink. Look for polyester options instead. Check my other reviews if you’d like to see the brand I found that worked way better for me.

I love all the pockets and, overall, the shorts seem very well-made. Also, they’re surprisingly comfortable. This is a great choice of shorts with fashionable flair. They will look fabulous on you. Just, go with your true size.

I am 5' 10'' tall, I weigh 175lbs and ordered an XL...fits great. I typically wear size 16-18 up in pants but closer to 16. I love how the legs are pinned up to show the fray at the bottom. But if you don't like them pinned up you can take them down easily. The bottom of the pants are pinned up by only a couple/few stitches in four places on each leg. These overalls have five pockets-two roomy pockets on each side in the front, two smaller pockets on the back and one large pocket (shaped like a back pocket) on the front. And did I mention these overalls have STRETCH? Matter of fact, there are four buttons on each side to undo to assist in putting these on but with so much stretch in this fabric I didn't need to undo even one button. Although these overalls don't look quite as sexy on me as they do the model, I do like them. And I plan on getting a lot of wear out of them.

I am 5’ 3” and weight 135lbs with an hourglass shape. I ordered an 8 since my waist is about 28 inch and my hips are 39 inch. If you’re short like me, either wear some tall heels or hem the pants. I expect to take off a good 4 inches off of mine. The material is cotton and upon first wear is stiff. I expect them to soften with use. The pants are nicely dyed with good contrast for what is a cow print and come with deep pockets! I can fit my entire hand down the front pockets and the silhouette remains flattering. I think the sizing is true to size just not accounting for petites. The pants take a nice fit to my bottom so that’s a win in my book. The rise of the pants is around the waist which many women like for the tummy control. Overall, would recommend for ladies 5 ft 6in and taller.

I am 5’ and I weigh 97-100 pounds it just depends! These pants around about 1 inch about my belly button and they fit like my Hollister (00) vintage mom jeans. Everyone said go up a size but I didn’t do that and I stayed my usual size which is a size 25. I was honestly thinking of sizing down but the reviews made me second guess everything but if I would’ve dozed down, they would’ve been too tight because these pants have little stretch. My Hollister jeans don’t really fit my waste fully fitted, but I don’t mind any of that because I do like to wear belts. I think if your pant size is your true pant size, for instance you’re a size 26 but you can only fit into a size 26 and not a Size 25, then maybe you should size up because the pants are were thick and aren’t very stretch at all. Also, these pants are VERY long but that was no surprise for me. It took me 6 rolls just to have it fit the leg of my length but you can always trim those down but of course the taller you are, the less amount of rolls you’ll have to do. But other than that, I do like the fit! I’m happy with the sizing I got and won’t be sizing down or up. I really hope this helps you!

I am 5' approx 125lbs. These fit so perfect! I got 5 colors, ALL IN SMALL REGULAR. I got the: Off white thick Black thick Khaki thin Pink thin Black coffee thick I'm glad I went with my gut/some reviews & didn't get 'short'. They all fit the same for the most part. Perfect around my waist with comfortable stretch left, but it wasn't loose it was fitted stretch left if that makes sense? Length was good on all of them except for the black coffee, that one needs to be hemmed and is super long. The rest fit perfect WITH heels on, which was my intent. If I want to wear them with any other shoe I will need to get them hemmed 100%. As far as thick vs thin goes, they feel like regular pants, neither thick or thin, I would have to wear the opposite to tell the difference. Although, I did wear the 'pink thin' out and you could see my cellulite LOL so maybe because it's thin. The black coffee is thick, the only one that does feel a bit warmer to me. The rest I would consider regular pants. The red heels in the photos are kitten heels, so not that high and the pink pants were above the ground. I tried to be very detailed! I hope this helps you! I definitely recommend so classy and chic!!

I am 5'1" 105lbs for reference. First impressions, these pants are AWESOME. I have a ton of harem and boho type pants but non that are this kind of material. It is almost like a cross between jersey and bathing suit material, is pretty thick compared to similar type pants and has a ton of stretch! The pattern/colors are exactly as shown in the listing. I absolutely LOVE these pants but alas! I cannot give them 5 stars, They are very long and are also too big. The elastic on the waist band is not very tight. The bottom seem where the band meets the pants is the tightest part of the entire waist band and even that to big to stay up. The first photo is where I think and would like the pants to sit, however, the second photo is where they fall. I cannot keep them any higher. They fall down when I walk, not completely, but enough where I caught myself constantly pulling them up. The third picture is me on my tip toes. You can see how the length is pretty perfect at that height but you can see by the other photos how it sits just standing regular. Because of the length, I was also constantly stepping on the bottoms which also pulled them down as well. I am washing them now so I will see if they shrink at all. Hoping they do but I have a feeling they won't due to the material. As is, I really need an XS or XXS in these pants to wear comfortably. If the waist band was actually tight and held them up securely, I could def wear a size small out of the house. But, as is, I can't see myself being able to wear these pants outside due to the constant falling down. So if you are more petite like me, I would pass on these unless they add smaller sizes.

I am 5’2 inches tall with a 27.5 inch inseam (crotch to floor). I ordered size M because sizing chart referenced inseam at 27in. When I tried on the pants, they dragged the floor (even with heels). Confused, I check listing (again) and measure myself (see pics) to discover the inseam measurement is 29.4 inches. Color is a very nice (classic) deep navy blue, the high rise waist measurement is accurate and very comfortable. Well positioned pleating allows for classic and traditional trouser fit (not too full in buttock/hip). 100% poly fabric is cheap, very lightweight and retains static electricity (clinging to legs and attracting lint and hair). It is disappointing that the mfg and vendor failed to communicate and ensure sizing chart reflected accurate information to the end user consumer. The trousers are low priced reflective of their low value and unworthy of the time and money it would take to hem. I will be returning.

I am 5'2" and around 150lb, typically between a M/L depending on fit; these I got a Large and an very happy. These pants have a button fly, which can sometimes be tricky to find the proper waist fit, but the back of these pants have an elastic waist that is perfect for a 'custom' and comfortable fit! The length of the pants is about 28" inseam, which hits me right at pant length but will be more of a culotte fit for someone taller. The waist has belt loops, as well as a removable 2-strand tie belt accessory. The fabric itself is a little rough, which caused me to remove one star; it's light and wrinkles just like linen, but is not quite as smooth in texture as I'd have hoped. Overall, these are decent quality for the price point, and very wearable summery/warm weather pants. I'm pleased with them and would recommend these pants, especially to petites looking for an inseam that won't drag on the ground.

I am 5’2”, 142 pounds and very pear shaped. I ordered a medium. Length is perfect! I didn’t want cropped pants, so these are the perfect petite length. The fit is comfy given my large hips, thick thighs, and will be comfy and flattering around my IBS flare ups. Fabric is actually flattering! I tested a bunch of linen pants at various shops recently and didn’t like the thin, see through linen (again, sensitive about my big hips.). These are a little thicker, and while the fabric is a little rougher (I suspect they’ll soften in the wash with some fabric softener), they’ll wear much better. And, the pricing here really can’t be beat for the quality. I ordered the striped navy blue. Navy is super dark (my lighting in pics is bad, sorry!), so the pinstripes pop nicely. I’m considering ordering another pair before my upcoming trip! These will travel so well, I can already tell. In a nutshell- yes, buy them.

I am 5’3”, 155lbs. This pair is a size 8 (I am always able to wear a size 8 in Levi Signature - as they all run big IMO). The waist measure 15.5 inches flat, the inseam is 27 inches (ankle length, if you are taller than 5’4 these are gonna be short, almost a perfect length for me at 5’3”), 11.5 inch rise, the leg opening is 14 inches. They are a mid-weight cotton blend with plenty of stretch. The rise is long, maybe a little too long, so the crotch hangs a little low if you don’t keep them pulled up. They are a nice slim straight style, that are a pretty classic cut that will likely always be “in-style” They are comfy. I have washed themed and they really didn’t seam to shrink and they have done a decent job of holding their shape throughout the day. Overall a solid pair of not expensive jeans, great for wearing around the house, but definitely don’t do anything to enhance your figure; like you don’t put them on and think “wow, these look great” it is more like “wow, these are a perfectly serviceable pair of jeans”. I mean for $23 you can’t go wrong really.

The fabric registers as a very 70's, thin polyester. VPL is a real issue, so exercise caution with your choice of undergarments. You may also want to size up for a looser fit, as these are very fitted and stretchy. I took two stars off because for this price (about $35 at the time of my writing) these are not cheaper than what I might find at a store specializing in casual basics, and those stores would have higher quality materials and craftsmanship. The tag on these says "do not put in the dryer" so they seem like they won't hold up.

I am 5’4”, 140lbs and I got this in a size medium. My legs are about 34 inches long. My waist is about 26 or 27 inches depending on how much I’ve eaten and my hips are about 40 or 41 inches at the moment. I usually struggle to buy jeans, but this pair is very comfortable and hugs. My curves the way it’s supposed to unlike most jeans and it doesn’t have that weird gaping space in the back. I could wear these with or without a belt. They’re comfortable and stretchy. I’m posing funny in 2 pictures because I wanted to show how much they easily stretch. I got them in four different colors, but I don’t recommend the white pair if you’re a perfectionist. The white pair collects too much fat is and hair to it. It happened to me at work with a scarf. That’s why I gave it 4 stars. If you go by the size chart, then it is true to size and like I said it stretches so it has gift to it. Not trying to be rude but whoever said they run small was in denial about their measurements. They’re as comfortable as my cotton jeggings from Uniqlo. I recommend these jeans especially if you’re curvy like me.

I am 5'4", rather athletic build, and not much of a booty.😭 I hover between 145-150lbs. I bought a medium, which was what was suggested. I wear anything from a size 6 to a 10 in bottoms, although 8 is my overall average. These are VERY high- waist on me, but I have a short torso so that might be why. They aren't as loose fitting at I thought they would be, but they aren't tight. The waistband is very stretchy so don't panick when you first pull them out the bag. I thought they were child's pants at first! I bought the army green color and it is much darker than what is usually considered army green. It's almost like what would be called a "forest green". The pockets are fine although they land lower on my butt than I would like. Note that the little front patch is just that--a patch of material that is sewn on, not an actual pocket. I am 5’5 and around 165 lbs. I purchased this because I love jumpsuits but didn’t expect much. Well, I love the way it looks! Unfortunately, I sized up as per the reviews saying that it had no give and needed a size bigger if I had curves. It was not the case for me. The waist elastic was a bit loose and there was some gaping around the hips as you can see in the picture, so I sent it back and purchased a size M (pictures are of a size L). The fabric is really nice, thicker denim and looks like it will stretch out with time so I think the smaller size will be more flattering. It had a bit of a scent on arrival so you will need to wash it before wearing it. The pants touched the top of my feet (a bit longer than ankle length). It has buttons and not snaps. The top is a bit bigger than the bottom (another reason why I decided to go down a size). But the construction is very nice. If you like jumpsuits and the 90s look, this is for you! Update: I ordered the M Size and it is a bit tight. Such a bummer! Since I’m between sizes, I’m keeping the smaller one and hoping it will feel better once the extra Christmas weight goes away 🫣

I am 5’5 and weigh 140lb. I was sitting on the fence about whether to get a medium or small and ended up choosing the Small size. I’m really happy I did because a medium would have been too baggy in the legs for form fitting leggings. The top is stretchy enough that they don’t cut into my mid section. I’m really happy with the fabric as well. They don’t feel itchy or cheap, the shine is subtle and they don’t make noise when you walk. The inside is smooth like silk. Great leggings.

I am 5'5-3/4"inches tall, waist size 31", hip size 41" and weigh 141 pounds. I usually wear a U.S. size 10 -12 pant. I ordered a large and it fits perfectly. I am high waisted so I always wear my blouses outside my pants. The fabric is 92% Polyester and 8% Spandex. The first photo shows me without shoes. In the other photos I am wearing a walking shoe with a 1-1/2" heel. The crotch is not tight and the pants are extremely comfortable. If you wear your tops tucked in all lines will be revealed. It hangs very nicely, is not too thin, and perfect for Spring through Fall. Suitable for casual or dressy occasions. I recommend going a size larger than you usually wear. I also bought the same pant in black. I machine washed them both in cold water and hung dry with no shrinkage whatsoever.

I am 5’5” 145 lbs and size 8 fit me very well. They are a tiny bit snug around the waist, but that’s because I need to lose a few pounds. They are otherwise comfy, they have a nice stretch without looking like those yoga pants that look like jeans, I hate those! I want my yoga pants to look like yoga pants and my jeans to look like jeans. The color is nice and quality seems great, I’m glad I bought these!

I am 5’5” 175 - I ordered an XL - these fit perfect!! They are stretchy and very comfortable, perfect length. They will need to be ironed. I haven’t owned shorts I felt this comfortable in for a long time! Buy these!! I’m getting another color!

I am 5'5" 180 and have large thigh - so it is always hard to find good jeans. I wish the waist was a bit higher on these but otherwise nice fit....not sure about the claim of but lifting. I am keeping them and will need a belt since I had to size up for my thigh. I would recommend.

I am 5'5" 180 and have large thigh - so it is always hard to find good jeans. I wish the waist was a bit higher on these but otherwise nice fit....not sure about the claim of but lifting. I am keeping them and will need a belt since I had to size up for my thigh. I would recommend.

I am 5’5” and 127 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 or 27 in jeans, medium bikini bottoms, and small in shirts. I was really torn which size to go with and decided on the medium recommended by the size chart. Let’s just go ahead and get the negative out of the way. When I think of “boyfriend jeans” I expect straight and loose legs but fits at the waist. The legs of these jeans are not baggy enough to be considered true boyfriend jeans. This pair in particular is quite a bit more fitted than the model photo. I would classify this style as distressed relaxed leg. Now let’s move on to judging the material. These are soooo stretchy. I wore them for a full day of running around town and they never lost shape or loosened up. They look tight in my photos but in real life the waist line does have some gapping at the spine and is barely touching my abdomen. Even though the size chart recommended a small, I am extremely glad I went with the medium. I think these jeans are stretchy enough that the small would have fit but they would have been super tight skinny jeans. The material of the light blue is slightly scratchy before a run through the washer. I don’t trust them to survive many rounds through the dryer but they did survive a tumble on the low heat/delicate cycle because I was trying to soften them up a bit. I will probably hand dry in the future to keep the fraying in tact. The paint splatters on the jeans are white and very much paint (not bleach) so they could flake off over time. Recommendation: Cheap enough to be worth a shot. The material is thin, stretchy, and ripped so these will make great summer jeans. Don’t expect them to be really loose unless you are willing to size up but keep in mind that the waist may be too big for you if you do size up. This pair of jeans just became my go-to for weekend road trips because they are stretchy enough for the car but also cute. I was comfortable and cool enough wearing them for a full day in the Texas heat.

I am 5’5” and 127 lbs. I order my usual small and it is true to size. The length isn’t quite long enough to offer full backside coverage. The buttons are metal and functional. The flannel fabric is 7/10 on the soft scale. The “denim” is more like a canvas and is also softish with nice flexibility. The colors in the plaid pattern of the flannel are cream, black, and mustard which makes the shacket easy to match. The green is just a little brighter than army green. Quality is excellent and this will make a perfect layering jacket for fall! Lightweight and just relaxed enough to be considered casual and oversized.

I am 5'5" and about 150 lbs. I grew out of a size 3 a couple months ago but haven't found another size that fits super great, so I went for a 4 with these. They fit pretty much perfect, but were maybe a tad too loose for my liking in the upper thigh/crotch area. I washed and dried before my first full wear. Unfortunately they didnt tighten up at all after the first wash and dry which was a bummer. They seemed to be the type of jeans that get a little bit looser as you wear them throughout the day. Nothing drastic but noticeable to me. That's the only downside. The color is nice and fit is perfect for the most part, thin, flexible material.

I am 5’5” and weigh 127 lbs. I bought the size 8 in black which fit true to size. At 72 years of age I wasn’t sure if this style would be flattering on me but decided to try them anyway. The last time I wore “mom jeans” was in the 1970’s! I didn’t notice any offensive odor like some of the other people noted in their reviews. They fit snug but will need to be hemmed up about 1.5”. I have not removed the tags or washed them yet but I don’t believe they will shrink. They have plenty of sideways horizontal stretch and they sit right at the waistline. I would buy these again, maybe in a darker blue jean color.

I am 5'5" and weigh 140. I purchased size 8 and they fit very well. I might could size down for tighter fit. I like the quality and color is perfect for the price! I am 5’6.5”, about 143-145 lbs, with a slightly athletic/muscular build. I tend to wear a 26/27 in pants and the medium fit me nicely. I also tried a size small (also pictured) but they were too tight in the mid thigh. Quick review version: pant material is nicer and thicker than expected, love that the waist band is smooth and not elastic. Nice length and overall shape. I can wear these with a pair of sneakers and the ends won’t drag. Longer/in depth: as mentioned, I have a bit more muscle, especially in the thighs. I first bought the small based on reviews and size guide. The waist fit me well, but the actual leg part was a bit too tight around my mid thigh. My pictures show me in the medium and small. You can see that the small is more tight in the thigh which causes a bit of buckling/a pouch to form at the top. The medium stays flowy through the leg. The waist on the medium is not too much larger and still fits me fine. These pants are good for cold weather or cold offices, but you could also probably wear them with a t shirt and not be too hot.
I am 5'6" 130 lbs. Size 4 fit well and were flattering. I did cuff the leg because that is the look I prefer with this type of jean.

I am 5'6" 145lbs and although I wasn't completely hating this outfit, I wasn't loving it either. I am returning because I am picky, not because it was a bad jumper. Material was comfy, I got a size large but I am usually a medium. I think the length is good for flats and maybe a short heel.

I am 5'6" 178 lbs and curvy. My waist is small but my hips are large. I have a hard time finding jeans that fit my hips and legs but aren't too big in the waist. These fit me perfectly! They are stretchy, soft, and feel like they were made for my body. I'm shaped like a real woman. I usually wear a size 14 so I ordered a 15 and 13 in these jeans. The 15 fits great but after wearing them a few hours tend to stretch out and become a little loose around my waist. The 13s fit a tiny bit snug around the waist when first putting them on, but within a few hours stretch into the perfect fit. I was skeptical about buying jeans online but figured since I could return them for free, I'd take a chance. So glad I did!! I ordered 4 more pairs last night! I will try to add pics later. Also the colors and washes of the jeans are true as advertised. I will never buy another brand of jeans again. The material is great quality and I feel as though these jeans will last a long time. I am used to wearing scrubs a LOT so if I say an article of clothing is comfortable you can guarantee they are extraordinarily comfy!

I am 5‘6” and 139 lbs. I bought a medium and they were way too big. Sent them back and got a small. I’m usually a small but sized up because of the reviews. i am 5'6" around 200 pounds and i was on the market for baggy jeans that dont take away from my curves. I LOVE THESE, i feel like it wraps my waist and butt so perfectly while still being baggy on the legs and calves which further make them so flattering. easy to dress up and down

I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

I am 5’6”, 123 lbs, and normally a size 4 in pants. I chose the medium based off the waist measurement on the size chart and I’m glad I did. The waist has elastic so there is some give if you are between sizes. The material is not stretchy so I would recommend double-checking the hip measurements before ordering. The legs are wide and full length on me. I ordered the “khaki” which is actually very light yellow with no tan tones. The material is thin but not as revealing as expected during wear. I would prefer a darker color so I wouldn’t have to worry about under things as much. My review photos were taken after my initial try-on after removing the pants from the jar. I had not washed nor ironed but wanted to get pictures posted to show the thinness of the khaki aka light yellow. I like the fit and feel of these cotton summer pants. It’s nice to have a loose and breathable material for the Texas summers. Like most linen pants, these wrinkle easily. The color is my biggest gripe but that’s personal preference.

I am 5’6”, 165lbs, I bought a W31. Fits great, stretchy and comfortable, thick material but not too thick. I just had a baby so needed to buy bigger clothes. It helps hide my flabby belly. I probably could have bought 1 size smaller, but I didn’t want them tight.

I am 5’7, 155 pounds and I ordered a size Large in the color Black. I go back and forth typically in sizes medium and large in pants. Sometimes I am a size 6 but often I am a size 8. These fit me tighter at the waist and hips and then are a straight leg with the slightest gradual flare going all the way down to give a tiny amount of wideness at the bottom. The length is perfect if I wear a flat shoe but anything higher they will be too short. I like to wear a chunky block sandal or something with a heel when I wear black pants like this and unfortunately I will not be able to do that. Other than that, these are extremely comfy and I love them!

I am 5’7, 155 pounds and I ordered a size Large in the color Black. I go back and forth typically in sizes medium and large in pants. Sometimes I am a size 6 but often I am a size 8. The large fits me in the waist extremely well. These pants are made out of 80% Nylon and 20% spandex so they are very stretchy and soft all over. One of the comfiest black dressier pants I now own! The waist is elastic and although it looks like it buttons up in the front, it is only there for visual purposes. These fit me tighter at the waist and hips and then are a straight leg with the slightest gradual flare going all the way down to give a tiny amount of wideness at the bottom. I wouldn’t consider these wide leg pants though. The only thing I wish I could change about these pants is the length. This is a normal problem for me though. The length is perfect if I wear a flat shoe but anything higher they will be too short. I like to wear a chunky block sandal or something with a heel when I wear black pants like this and unfortunately I will not be able to do that. Other than that, these are extremely comfy and I love them!

I just wanted something slightly more roomy which the medium gabe me without looking or feeling too big.
