Highly recommend for anyone looking for comfy, stylish shorts post-baby!

I bought these slacks for work. I needed a pair that were not skinny. They are comfortable and professional looking, but needs ironing after washing. I’m 5’7”, 130 lbs and size 8 fits fine in length and hip area. I do need a belt due to the waist being a little too largeUpdate: Be careful ironing! When I placed the barely heated iron on the pants, it burned the material. Now the pants are useless!

I bought these to wear to an Elton John concert. The fit was great, except they were a bit too long for me as I am only 5’4”. That happened a lot with pants for me and NOT a manufacturer’s issue. They looked like the photo when they came, and I was happy about that. Perfect for the occasion!

I bought these to wear to work (because real jeans are not to comfortable to sit in all day). These stretch jeggings are super comfortable and the material is very soft and stretchy. I think they are true to size and being 5'8" & 180 lbs, I should have just went with my usual XL size. I'm still happy with my purchase because I bought them for comfort. Good jeggings for the price.

I bought these trousers to wear to work and wear with a black top that I had just purchased. The trousers fit right at my ankle. I am 4 feet 11.5 inches tall and 144 pounds I usually wear a size 8. I’m a full size 8 presently so I got the size 10 and they fit like an 8 they are a little snug in the midsection but they work. They sit above my belly button. I don’t consider them a skinny pants they are more like a straight leg pants like someone in a previous review had mentioned. I like the style and fit enough to purchase another color at some point.

I bought these when I was 26 weeks pregnant. This was definitely a must-have. My regular ones even with the elastic band is just not comfortable whenever I sit. The downside to this is there's not enough pockets (all the pockets are on the right side)and it does not come in petite. So I need to hem the bottom. But this is very comfortable.I'm 5'2, and now 153lbs. I fit a medium with room to move. I had it for a two weeks and already notice there's a wear in thigh area. So I bought another one to alternate.
I bought this for my daughter for our family pictures. She’s 5’6” 130 and athletic build and it fit her perfectly!

I bought this for the Western Wear portion of a pageant and OMG this dress was not only flattering, but so comfy! (For context, I ordered an XS and I’m 5’1, 115 lbs.
I bought this jacket to embellish it. I absolutely love it. This $50 jacket now looks like a $350 jacket I love it I am 5”10 and the jacket hits right around my knees

I bought this jean jacket for a concert I went to and it was perfect! I was scared of what size to buy but glad I got the size that I did. It fits just right. Not too small and not too tight! I am 5’8 203 lbs with a bust size of 43 and I bought a XL.
I bought this on sale and thought pink would be a fun summer color. I am 5’1 and even in the small it was long on me, so I ended up cutting them to give them a more cropped look.

I bought this outfit for graduation and a few other outfits and I absolutely love this one!! It is so comfortable and flattering!! I am 5’3 and weigh 195 and an xl was perfect!! I recommend this outfit to everyone!!

I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!

I bought this to wear to the Zach Bryan concert. It is just what I was looking for! TTS (I am 5’10”, 160 lbs, ordered a medium). It is comfortable. Can be worn with a tank or tshirt or long sleeved shirt underneath. The denim is not stiff. Length is perfect.

I bought this when it was on a lightening deal. I like it, and I’ll probably buy more colors if they go on sale again. The brown color is perfect for fall, though and will be very versatile with lots of outfits. It’s kind of a spandex material but it’s soft and has a little warmth to it. I love the neckline! I purchased a small. It is definitely tight through the midsection area and I may have liked a medium better but I like small enough to keep it instead of deal with a return. I’m postpartum and my size has been changing a lot but I typically wear a small-medium so I’d say it’s true to size. 5’4”, 130lbs, 27” waist.

I bought two different pairs- an xs and a s. The XS was more "fitting" but then the waist was more snug than I would have liked. The S was a looser trouser look. Also, the coloration from the images isn't exact especially for the brown, it's much more "desaturated." They are stretchy and soft. Almost like fancy sweatpants. I have yet to wash, but due to their composition, I assume the will have the tendency to shrink. The pockets are deep, the quality seems alright. It's an easy to pull fabric, so gentle cycles. No issues with the zipper and button. Opaque, decent length, lightweight. I am 5'6, 128lbs and they sit 2-3 inches above my ankle - I'm pretty used to that. I'll update my review after a few wears.

I bought two pairs of these jeans for my daughters. They both fit great and were comfortable. I love that they're 99% natural fiber. I was also so glad to find 30" inseam here on Amazon because I couldn't find that short of a length at any of our local western/farm stores. All in all, great jeans and so super cute.

I bought two pairs to try on. My body: 5’2”, 130lbs, 29” inseam, Typically Size 2-4/XS-S. Pair 1: Army Green, Size S, Regular Length, Thick Fabric. Pros: Standard “dress pant” thickness, great length for me to wear with either flats or small heels/wedges. Beautiful green color, nice earth tone. Cons: Tighter in the center, felt like they hugged my body between the inseam and belly button tighter than the Khaki pair. Only two front belt loops, no back belt loops. Button front closure, not a “hidden button” like the Khaki pair. Would prefer to wear a belt with this pair to disguise that. Pair 2: Khaki, Size S, Shorter Length, Thin Fabric. Pros: Loved the thin, moveable fabric. Pockets and elastic waist sat flatter than than on the green pair. Hidden button closure looks better than the exposed button. Stitching looks better quality. Cons: Too short for me to comfortably wear with the wedges. Khaki color is not as flattering on me as the green. Overall: If the Army Green color was in stock in the thin fabric in my size, I’d order that instead. I much prefer the hidden button closure of the thin fabric and the structure seems to be better quality. They also look like a higher end product in person. However, due to the length and color not being right for me, I am only keeping the Green color and wearing those for my event instead of the khaki. I would recommend these pants overall and they are a great item to have in the closet! The biggest downside is that there are only two front belt loops. For the thin fabric with the hidden button closure it is less of a problem, but the exposed button really could use a belt to pull the outfit together. Overall I didn’t notice a size difference between the two, but the thin fabric felt like it was more flattering. I’ll probably wear shaping shorts under these just for my own comfort. Definitely need to wash (instructions say cold, gentle cycle or soak for a short time, hang dry) and steam out the wrinkles from the packaging before wearing!
I bought XXL, I am 5" 6' and around 215 lbs. Fabric is very soft. Not see through.

I brought these back in 2022 and never wore them because I thought I couldn’t pair them with anything. I was so self conscious and was always worried about what others thought of me. After finding my style, my confidence and loving for myself again, I’ve revisited the pants and ABSOLUTELY LOVE THEM! I am 5’7, 175lbs (I was about 155 when I first order them 😆) and got a size Medium and they still fit perfectly!!! Would recommend!!

I buy alot of clothes from Amazon and base my purchases and sizing from reading reviews. I’m 5’3, 195lbs, i carry all my weight in my stomach and back. I don’t have curvy hips and my chest area and hips are the same size. I purchased a size Large. I wore shape wear underneath because i like to not have to worry about a bra or underwear. I wear a one piece shape wear that is not tight but smoothes out any rolls i have. The jumpsuit has no stretch but its not constricting either. I thought the large would be to tight too at first i knew that sizing up might be to long for me. I washed the jumpsuit on a gentle cycle and dried on a low heat setting. Honestly I would purchase again but going to the bathroom took me a bit longer. Also the button in the front was great but i did need to pin the front since it would open up.

I by no way have thicker calves. If you do or have muscular calves these will not work well. 5’ 6” 135lbs, ordered medium and the fit was perfect other than the calf area. When I sat down then stood up the legs got stuck up. I didn’t want to mess with pulling them down every time I moved so these are going back. Would have kept them otherwise.

I can fit anywhere between a medium to xL so I got a large to be safe but it is very baggy even for someone with a bigger butt. I’m 5’3 like 160 & it felt very roomy around the crotch & pretty long. The best part is the waist fits, but the thighs and hips are not too tight! The length is also just right. I am 5'3", 134 lbs, with a 29" waist, 38" hips and 22" thighs. I got the 6 short in light blue, but I could see maybe squeezing into the next smallest size if you want a tighter fit. Hopefully they last a long time!

I can’t believe how luxurious these feel. They feel extremely comfortable & high quality. Fell in love as soon as I felt it! They’re stretchy, thick & wear nicely. I’m 5’3” 160 pounds 31 weeks pregnant. Pants stretch perfectly over my stomach & the top will grow with my belly. Only con is there’s no pockets.

I can't say enough about these pants! I LOVE them! They are super comfortable. The rips are all all in the right location. These pants are also very flattering to my figure. I'm 5'5 225lbs...I purchased a size 2X...

I can't say enough good things about these jeans! They look and feel like real jeans, not like jeggings. They have a tummy control panel that comes down about 3 inches from the waistband. It completely flattens my fupa and lower stomach pooch!! And I can still comfortably eat in them. I will say, the more booty you have the more difficult it is to pull them up bc of the stomach compression part. I have 42 inch hips and thick, relatively muscular thighs. I got a size 8 in these which is average for me. I'm an 11 in juniors sizes, if that's useful. It takes some effort to get them over my butt, but once I get the compression fabric up over my thighs and butt I only have to do the *jumps while pulling pants up* once to get them the rest of the way up. 😂 The waistband is smooth and invisible under a thin t-shirt, whereas most jeans cause weird lines where the button and zipper are. They're stretchy enough that there's no gap at my lower back. They do have to be pulled up pretty regularly for me when moving around. I think I might also get a pair in size 10 to wear on more active days.

I completely love these vintage jeans! I try to keep my expectations at bay when ordering jeans online because finding the right fit can be challenging. These far exceeded my expectations! I ordered my usual size and they are a little bit big but based on the fabric content, I expect that they may shrink a little bit over time so I think I still made the right choice. The waist is high, yet comfortable with a good amount of stretch when sitting. It did roll on me a little bit after sitting for a long period of time but I suspect that is because they are a little loose in the waist. I'm 5'4" and these are just a tiny bit too long but perfect with a bootie that has a smaller heel. A taller person might find these to be ankle-length. The zipper is sturdy, the pockets are nice and the stitching is of good quality. Lastly, these are soooo comfortable. I'm a happy customer!

I could go for an extra 2" or more in length, but overall, these are really great. They're very soft, reasonably well made, super beachy and summery. I look forward to wearing them many more times this summer. If you are 5'9" or taller, you'll be hard-pressed to get them to be simultaneously high-waist and long enough! It's very close.

I could not find ANY sizing information for these jeans anywhere. I even went to the LifeShe store. I guessed an XL and I think they fit pretty much the way I wanted them to so I guess I was lucky. I'm 5'7" and about 170 with 43" hips. They are loose and comfy. Also be aware that, while there is NO spandex (so NO give) these are also NOT 100% cotton (as they are described). The inner label clearly states 12% polyester and, while I really don't care about that, I wish the description had been accurate. All in all they are nice jeans for everyday

I couldn’t decide between this or the competing Levi’s jacket but ended up deciding on this after reading it was a little more stretchy and less boxy. I got this in the darker denim and I looove it!! The price is amazing compared to what else is out there. It is very comfortable, not too heavy not to lightweight, and the fabric is very movable and flexible. No problem rolling up the sleeves. I like the length on this one, too. Many I’ve tried are too short but this one isn’t. Bring on fall!

I decided that I am SO over the skinny jeans, and went looking for something different—and I found these wonderful barrel jeans! I had never heard of such a thing, but I LOVE the fit, the feel, and the look!❤️ I am 5’6” and I usually order a small, but this time I decided to go with a medium, according to the measurements on the chart. I am so glad that I did, because the fit is exactly the way I want it, without being loose in the waist. The fabric is just right, not flimsy, not stiff as a board. The color is worn, with dark around the stitching. I love it! The stretch is remarkable, and so comfortable. These are going to be my new favorite pair of jeans. I highly recommend.😊

I decided to purchase a large based off of reviews and I'm happy I did although I believe a medium would have fit too. I am 5'8" and 150lbs and I am typically a true medium in clothing. I have broader shoulders than most females and my frame is your average mom build. The large gives me plenty of room to wear a light sweater underneath and still button. The length of the jacket hits at my waist right above my hip and the sleeves come all the way over my wrist!! It's the perfect medium wash jean color and I'm looking forward to wearing this all fall long!

I decided to take a chance on these jeans since I could return them for free. They are being returned. They fit okay. They look pretty good in the side view pic, right? Now that I see that pic, maybe a little baggie in the butt but I don't want skin tight jeans. But now look at the front. What are those bulges on the side? I guess if you want to buy these, it is your call. I am 5'2" and weigh between 124-128 and the length was perfect. If not for those "wings" I would have kept them.

I decided to try out baggy high waisted cropped jeans for a change. The leopard pattern is great, subtle, still cool. I like the fabric a lot, it has no stretch to it, but it is soft enough, really sturdy, thin, structured. The pants are a bit too cropped for me, but I am tall with long legs (5’9”), so that is expected, and with high heeled pointed boots would look elegant. The problem for me is the top. To look dressy and elegant, these pants should be high-waisted, and they hit at the top of the hips giving an overall boxy shape. For reference, I wear size M (6-8, waist 27” or 28-29” when the pants are mid-rise).
I DEFINITELY RECOMMEND 10/10 WIUKD DEFINITELY BUY AGAIN! I’m 5’0 and small fits perfect for me I’m glad I ordered it and I REALLY recommend it!!!

I didn’t have high hopes but I want these in every color!! I’m 5”2” approx 110lb . I have a 27” inseam and these are still a bit cropped although I like to roll a little as you see in the picture . This midrise is perfect on me, as I can not wear high rise! The small is perfect!

I didn’t have huge expectations for these jeans but they are amazing! They are exactly what I needed while I’m waiting for my post hysterectomy belly swelling to go down. I didn’t have the highest hopes for this jumpsuit. Being 5’10, I figured the torso would be too short, as well as the leg length. I also thought I’d be too big for a small. I was wrong! This jumpsuit was SO easy to put on. It fit like a glove and made me feel FABULOUS. I was able to dance & do a kick line in this outfit. Got several compliments & plan to wear again. Plus, I can wear 3 inch heels with this outfit & it’s still not too short. 😊

I didn’t like these because just in general, the cut and measurements were really off for me. But I will go into detail as to why below. For reference I tried sizes 4 and 6 short (no difference except 6 was a little looser in the thigh). PROS: - Length is perfect for someone shorter (I’m 5’2) CONS: - Gaping in the waist - Loose in the crotch (causes lots of folds & bunching of fabric) - Tight in the thigh (but this was minor con)

I didn’t realize that the product name had “sheer” in it because all the review pics looked opaque, but holy cow!! I’m wearing emerald green panties and you can very clearly see them. It’s a shame because they’re made out of a really comfy material & fit perfectly (I’m 165lbs, 5’4” & ordered a large). I will not be keeping them, however, for reasons self-evident.

I do like these pants but they do run a little small. They have a lot of stretch so not a big deal but be aware incase you're on the border, order up. Material is nice but not too thick. It does makes a whooshing noise between the thighs so just be aware of that if you have thicker thighs. Ordered my normal size large. Reference 5'7 178lbs

I do love these pants. Ordered in a small and they fit perfectly on the waist portion but long in length but was expected because I am only 5ft 2in. I'll have to get these hemmed. The material has some weight to it so it's not too thin. It is kinda see through but it is expected of this material - I wore nude colored undies to correct that. I do love this two piece a lot! It doesn’t look like the advertised photo but it is still cute. Thin denim material so I think good for summer. NO STRETCH so keep that in mind. I ordered the medium and it was too tight for me. I am 5’5 at 150lbs. I think the large would fit well but I like to have some space in my clothes so if I ordered again I would get the XL. Could be cute for pairing maybe tights and a long sleeve for winter. I did do a return though. Still not sure if I would buy again at this price point. Overall, good quality and very cute. It did take about a month to get to me.

I do not like trying on pants or jeans I avoid it at all costs they are very tricky to fit on my curvy very short 61 year old body. However these were a winner ! I purchased the F'AB BODY SCULPT SKINNY style. These jeans have lots of stretch, the waistband in particular is so comfortable and it is high enough to cover muffin tops. They are intended to be ankle length but I have to hem up about 2" as I am 5'2". The sculpting darts and elastic panels really do work ! I am size 8 but originally purchased size 10 based on some reviews and I was nervous that an 8 would be too tight. The 10 fit but after wearing a few days the waistband loosened up and I was constantly hiking them up. I switched that out for a size 8 . I recommend trying your usual size in the SCULPT SKINNY style.

I do not understand all the tall women raving about how long these pants are. I’m 6’1”, 37” inseam (33-34” jegging inseam) and these were easily 2-3” too short for me. I was skeptical based on the description, but the reviews persuaded me to order. I got the 6 (my normal size) Long and they did fit fine other than length. Wish Amazon would add an extra long option!
I don’t even blame the pants anymore I blame my body. They’re the first pants that fit in six pairs but they just look awful. 115 lbs 5’2, short torso

I don’t know in all my years of ordering off Amazon I’ve ever really LOVED a piece of clothing. These are a must have. I ordered a large. I typically wear L/XL 12. I’m 5’1 they are just touching the ground. The material is nice the back of the waist is elastic. Will be buying in other colors

I don’t know what this brand is doing, but every pair fits amazingly. I’m size 14, 5’9”, 200 lbs, and bought an XL. The fit like a glove and are the perfect length. I ended up buying black and then another lighter wash. I’ve also purchased their other styles and LOVE them all, and for the price, they rival some of my other higher end pairs of jeans!

I don’t know why these pants have such bad reviews but I love them. I ordered them in cream and black. The black feels more fitted than the cream ones.They have a bit of space in the front but that’s fine if you wear a belt and pull them up higher. They’re in between high waisted and mid rise. I’ve tried a good variety of slacks and these are good quality. For reference I ordered a size medium. I’m 5’5, weighing 130 lbs.

I don't normally leave reviews, but I absolutely had to for these pants. I am a server at a very busy restaurant, in Florida where it gets hot as heck. I was out of work for 2 months thanks to covid shutting down all our restaurants, and well I gained some weight while sitting at home doing nothing 🤣🤣🤣. I was called to return to work on Memorial Day weekend and of course, my black work pants would not fit over my newly found, voluptuous body. I spent 2 days running from store to store, fitting rooms were closed and I was getting desperate. I bought and returned no less than 10 pairs to various stores. I found 2 pairs that fit decently, but I absolutely hated because they didn't fit right. I have a small waist, big hips, big thighs, etc., so I need more of a stretchy material. I gave in and hit Amazon figuring if I had to buy and return multiple pairs, I could at least do it in the comfort of my own home. Well, this was the 1st pair i ordered. I can absolutely say, i LOVE LOVE these! They fit perfect, stretch in all the right places, they are light enough so they aren't hot, so soft, unlike starchy, rough dress pants. If you have a job where you are required to wear black dress pants, I highly recommend these. For reference, sizing is spot on. I am 5'7", 165 lbs and typically wear a size 11 juniors/8 or 10 womens. I bought the Medium 31" inseam and they fit perfectly! I ordered 2 more pairs right away!

I don’t think anyone is loving the elastic band on these pants, but that is my only complaint. I like my jeans tight around my butt/thighs, which is hard because I don’t much have a butt or thighs. But I love this fit and they feel like wearing comfy sweatpants. I’m 5’7”, 115lbs, 27”Waist, 34”Hips, 18” Thighs and got an XS. There’s a bit of a loose area in front, but it doesn’t look weird and makes space for more curvy figures. The fleece does add a scotch of bulk, but I need all the bulk I can get on my skinny legs. The cut is tighter in the lower legs than the seat and thighs. Probably not great if you’ve got larger or athletic calves, but the stretch is fantastic. I put these in the dryer on low to tighten up just a little, no problems. The fleece isn’t terribly thick like another pair I have, but is still warm and super soft. The blue is a nice wash on the less faded side than some. I very much recommend these fleece jeans to pretty much anybody.

I don’t think I’ve worn corduroy pants since middle school, but I’m loving these. They are so, so cute on and I love the tapered leg and the cuff at the bottom. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs, and wear size medium (6-8), and according to their sizing, a medium is 8-10. However, the waist looked like it might be a little tight and I’m adverse to anything being tight there, so I sized up to a large. The waist fits great, I probably would have been uncomfortable in the mediums, but I don’t think you need to size up if you can handle it. These are not super long, but hit me right above the ankles and it looks cute. The material is thicker (fabric is 70% Chenille, 25% Polyester, 5% Elastane) but not that you’ll be sweating. These will be great for fall.

I enjoy wearing this a lot! It’s not long on me (thank goodness) and it still looks good with shoes. I bought size small with 29” and I am 151 pound at 5’4” so I hope this helps someone . It fits very well in the front and it’s not tight on my hips or butt. Doesn’t cause any muffin tops or ugly underwear see through. I like The fabric as well because it’s light and it’s still warm enough to wear it in 50 to 60° temperature. I like it so much. I’m going to buy two more pairs and different colors🤩🤩🤩🤩🤩

I expected these to be bulky but they’re not. Very comfortable. I’m 5’7 and they’re shorter than I’d like but not short enough that I’d return them. I’m 143 lbs and got the size 8 expecting them to stretch out. I would get another pair!

I expected these to fit like sweatpants. In the front they kind of do but in the back, they have a very slim fit. I thought this made them hang really nice, mire like dress slacks, but it might be challenging for someone who is bigger through the hips and buttocks region. Despite being a knit fabric there’s not a whole lot of stretch to the material. The material is very soft and comfortable. The pockets are amazing. There are six packets including two cargo on the sides small pockets on the back and deep pockets for your hands upfront. I washed these and they didn’t have any color fade or pilling. Seem to be wearing well. For the right figure, I think these are an excellent find. They were long enough for my 5 foot seven frame, and it would be ideal for someone who is slimmer through the hips and butt, frustrating if you’re bigger in those areas. A little poochy in the front like a sweat pant but overall very nice. I think they will be a comfortable option for travel- look nicer than sweatpants, have pockets for storage, and will be comfortable.
I feel beautiful in these pants. 5’1”, 125 lbs, 31 years old and I bought the 2 Short.

I feel like a frumpy old lady wearing these pants. They run large. My hip measurement is 40" and I got a size 8 (sizing down one because I read they run big) and they are still kinda loose, even after washing and drying. They are well tapered to the waist for me though, and my natural waist measurement is 25". As I walk, the pants bunch up, pulling away from my ankles. I'm 5'-5" and if the pants didn't bunch up, they would otherwise be the perfect length for wearing with flats. I think they are too short to wear with heels. The pants are extra loose at the knee. I would describe the fit as similar to relaxed fit jeans. The fabric is very stretchy, but also very thick yet lightweight at the same time, and prone to wrinkles. The fabric feels cheap. The pants are reasonably comfortable though.

I feel like some kind of crazy mad scientist in this shacket. I expected it to be a little bit big seeing as I'm typically an XS and this is a small (I'm 5'3, 100 lbs.). But it's also super lightweight and wrinkles so easily. The white one doesn't really look like the listing photos- I'm not sure how they edited them, but they made it look super defined and darker around the seams. In reality, it's a bright white and I had a really hard time styling it to make it look good and no matter what I did, I couldn't get the collar to lay down. This just isn't for me, but it does look cute in the other colors!
I feel like this is probably a very universally flattering dress. I got a large and I’m a size 10-12 US. 33 inch waist and 43 inch hips

I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

I felt compelled to write a review because there were none in my size range. I usually wear a 6R at 5'7" 140ish lbs, which is what I ordered. Something I noticed before ordering was that the measurements on the size chart for sizes 6 and 8 were the same. What? So how do I know what size to get? I usually get a 6, but knowing this brand is usually for younger people it felt like a risk. As you can see from the photos, it's a really snug 6. I did try these on at the end of the day when (for me) pants are tightest because of gravity 😂 I think that after some wear and washes, they will get softer. The material is a bit rougher than I usually like, with less give, but you get what you pay for. They will be good for cool weather. I also like the wash, I don't like huge lines on my hips and these aren't too in-your-face. (I would have opted for a darker wash but it wasn't available in my size.) So overall pretty good, I'll throw them in the rotation and pair them with a thrift store flannel to keep the dream of the 90s alive 😎

I figured these would be excellent pants to wear for work when I have to dress business casual. They are extremely comfortable and very well-made. The corduroy is excellent. My only issue is that I am only 4 foot 11 and I have a rather large pouch of fat in my lower stomach area (fupa) And I noticed that these pants seem to sort of accentuate that quite a bit I’m not sure if I wear some extra tight shape wear. It will help even it out and look a bit better. On another note, I am also short in the torso area so my pants tend to come up above my belly button even if they are not meant to be ultra high-rise so I do believe that has a lot to do with the issues on how these look on the other than that these are very great pants and I love the cinched part at the bottom of the pants so I don’t have to worry about them being too long like most others

I finally found a pair of jeans that fit me. It has been hard to find jeans to fit my thighs,hips, and bottom. These jeans are very comfortable, and I love that they have the warm fleece liner inside to keep you toasty warm. These jeans have a stretchy waist and they have the zipper and button that actually works to make these even better. Pockets are good size and fits your hand nicely. The material is nice and they are made well. The jeans are long on me,but only because I am very short. (5'1"). Be sure not to put in dryer and lay out to dry. Wash by hand or on delicate wash. These look great and feel great all around. My new number one pair of pants now.

I Finally found a pair of pants that fit the way I want. I really love this pants, fits great, feel comfortable, a little long for me but doesn't look bad, I'm 4' 10" and normally use 12-14 pants size. I'll get one in blue too. I just love them.

I finally found some black levis that fit. These are the blackest black levis I have ever seen. They are soft and stretchy and they fit great (My butt is lifted up). As I mentioned in another review (Levis 311 skinny plus, kabekona camo), I currently wear an 18 in Womens Levis 505 which run larger than most levis so I sized up to a size 22 to be safe. I probably could have gotten away with a size 20 with these but they are so comfortable (I don't want to take them off). However, I did not have to hold my breath to button them or wiggle into them, they just slid on, so I'm happy. I will also have to wear a belt with these which is always the case for me. For reference: I am 5"5 with thick hips, butt, and thighs and currently weigh around 260 and my stomach is not flat but it is smaller in proportion to the rest of my body.

I finally had an opportunity to wear this very cute, wide-legged jumper. Although the picture is not very flattering, it really is a cute & stylish piece. I wore it to my nephews graduation & got many compliments about my "on point" outfit. The wide-legged design sets it apart from other standard jumpers. I wear a size medium & it was true to size. I'm just about 5'7" & this fit well. I had to shorten the straps a bit, so if you are taller, this would work well. If you have a more petite frame, this may not work for you because the adjustable straps may not adjust to be short enough. This is a great option for anyone who loves to rock jumpers!

I first bought a pair in size 12 according to the measurements given. They were pretty big & long. But I loved how the style was & they have a great stretch! So, I ended up purchasing a size 8 & they fit perfect! I usually wear size 10 I am 5’3 & have 30” waist

I first bought a pair of these pants in the color "blue ice." Size small. (First picture) They fit so nice! I was like, "Yeah mama!" My butt looked awesome, unlike most maternity pants that kind of give you that saggy mom butt. I liked them so much that I bought another color, "flip sig." Also size small. Now these were too small and tight. As you can see in the second photo, it was like some crazy muffin top and not flattering at all. So I exchanged them for a medium and they fit great! Just like the first pair did in small. So I would say there are some size discrepancies between colors.For reference I am 5'7", 130ish and size 4 pre-pregnancy.

I first got these pants in size 6, which is my usual size (I'm 5'8", 120 lbs.); they fit, but I wanted them a little looser for comfort (I'm 71 years old and don't like them "skin tight"). I returned them for a size 8. They fit perfectly! Very sleek and sophisticated looking! I love the longer length also! I got a size 6 and the fit is pretty good. I typically go back and forth between size 4 and 6 on jeans depending on brand. Since I never owned this brand before, I took a shot at a 6. The fit is fine except the crotch depth and waist height is a little too deep for my figure, but that is typical on me with other brands where I get a size 6. All that said, I would guess the jeans are close to true to size.I am 5’8” and the jeans are long enough to wear, but only with flatter shoes. They fall to the back of my heal. I prefer a jean that almost touches the floor in the back, so these are about an inch to 1-1/2” shorter than I like. Once again, I find this problem with other brands so I don’t think this is a sizing issue, just a figure issue for me. (I’m kind of short waisted and long legged).The jeans are made well and I like the fabric. They feel like “broken in” jeans from the moment you put them on (a big plus for me). They feel nice on the skin. The fabric is also a nice weight - not too thin or too thick. I found them comfortable to wear. The waist stayed in place and they didn’t “bag” in any areas after a day of wearing. The color is perfect. I do like the straight leg cut of the jean. To me it’s much more comfortable than skinny jeans.I have never owned jeans with cargo pockets before, so this is something new. I have mixed feelings on the pockets. I think it’s just something I need to find the correct top to go with them and they will be fine.Overall, I think these are a great jean and run true to size. Five stars.

I got a size 7, but I probably could’ve gone another size up since it’s tight around the waist. I do have a scar on my stomach, but the button doesn’t pinch it so it’s not painful (I don’t know my hip size, but I’m around 120 lbs), but for someone else who has bigger hips or stomach, I would recommend going 2 sizes up. Keep in mind the waistline will be the tightest part compared to the rest of the jeans. Overall, the pants are actually fairly comfortable and the material isn’t scratchy at all. It’s somewhat stretchy. Some jeans can be irritating if you don’t shave your legs, but this pair is comfy regardless if you shave or not! It’s only been one day, and the zipper keeps riding down when moving around. I don’t know if it’s because the waistline of the jeans is too small for me or if it’s just a general issue, but it isn’t a big deal. Jeans are a big long too, since I’m 5’3 and it kind of scrunches up around the ankles, but that’s an easy fix! I don’t have hardly any curves and my butt is flat as can be, but the pants did give me some of a figure (not by much haha!). It also didn’t slide down while bending, so that’s a plus. I never wore high waist jeans before, but this might turn me over.

I'm 5'4" (163cm) and 130lbs (~60kgs). They are about the perfect fit and size for me, a tad bit loose on the calves so giving a loose skinny look. (I prefer the slightl loose compared to the skin tight jeggings look) They do give me a bit of a muffin top but I don't really mind as long as they don't either slip down as I walk around or squish me breathless. Band feels nice and secure and it didn't seem to give me the weird crotch. I will see how it wears after a wash but so far so good.

I got the olive ones mainly because in the ad photo they looked like they had a subtle vertical stripe texture. In person, they do have that texture and it's very pretty, enhancing a long silhouette. But guess what? As I removed them I saw that they have more pronounced striping of varying widths inside. So I turned them inside out and put them on, AND THEY ARE SO CUTE! Yes, I'm totally going to wear them this way. The details from the outside of the pants (stitching around real back and faux front pockets, waistband, fly, rivets) all show on the inside as well, I honestly don't think anyone could tell they aren't supposed to be worn inside out. OMG, are other colors of this legging striped inside? I have been looking everywhere for striped leggings. Most designers seem to think that legging stripes should be horizontal--you know, so that you look as much like a chubby toddler as possible. OK, so, standard features that other people who aren't as stripe-obsessed as I am might care about: 75% cotton very long-- I got the One Size; I'm 5'8", curvy muscular 140lbs, but there is plenty of length, scrunching at my ankles beautiful shading so that it's not just a bland olive smooth- and sleek-looking super-soft and comfortable stylish design great value for the price did I mention that they're striped on the inside and reversible?!

I got the one size leggings! My waist is about 36 inches, and the thickest area around my butt is 42 inches. They fit great and there was room to spare :) I’m a size 8 in jeans. I’m 5’9 and 165 lbs, and my inseam is a solid 30 inches. I have the leggings all the way over my belly button and there was still about an inch of room at the bottom so :) Notice the underwear line tho...

I got the pink/blue in a size large, and they fit great. I am a little worried about them shrinking in length after wash/dry, so I may opt to air dry. (I am 5'7'' 155lbs) These are fun to wear around the house, and maybe I will get brave and wear them out of the house!

I got the plum colored pants in size medium. I am 5'6" and I typically wear a size 4-6. The length on these pants was just right. I'm unsure how I feel about the fit of the pants on me, but I'd say these are comparable to a 2-4 sized pant. I wanted these to look like skinny jeans, but feel like leggings. For the most part, they accomplish that. The fabric is thin, soft, and stretchy, which is good for physical comfort, but also means that the fabric really conforms to your body and doesn't feel like it's hiding anything. For this reason, I'd personally stick to darker colors that might not show every dimple off.

I could’ve sized down I believe but the nature of my job requires to be comfortable and presentable so the loose fit is great. The pants material feels expensive and you are not able to tell that they aren’t a known brand..they are luxurious. Love love love it. I will get another color asap

I got the silver in size 4. I am 5' 3.5" 124# with a mom pooch (4 kids) and I love these pants!!! They are stretchy and have tummy control top type thing going on in the top part. I own 3 pairs already. Black, navy and silver, and I plan on getting more!!!!😍

I got the straight leg khaki pants in a medium. I’m 5”2 and 147 lbs and the pants fit well. I like the way they feel. I just don’t like the front pockets because the pants are so thin that you can see the lining of the pockets. I wear these pants with long shirts so you can’t see the pockets. I still like these pants because they are very comfortable.
